Japanese: １２月２７日　午後２時１１分 成歩堂法律事務所
English: December 27, 2:11 PM Wright & Co. Law Offices

Japanese: 御剣検事‥‥ なんで、あんなことを‥‥？
English: What was Mr. Edgeworth talking about...?

Japanese: 私が犯した罪の記憶‥‥。
English: A memory of a crime... that I committed.

Japanese: 殺人‥‥の記憶だ。
English: A memory... of murder.

Japanese: 御剣検事‥‥ まさか、誰かを‥‥？
English: Do you really think Mr. Edgeworth killed...?

Japanese: そんなこと、あるわけがないよ。
English: I don't believe it. Not Edgeworth.

Japanese: たしかに、あいつは今、何かの 記憶に苦しんでいるけど‥‥。
English: Some painful memory has been troubling him recently...

Japanese: 誰かの命をうばうなんて‥‥ あり得ない！
English: But he'd never take someone's life! Never!

Japanese: なるほどくん‥‥。
English: Nick...

Japanese: よお！　元気かねショクン！
English: Yo! How's everyone doing?

Japanese: どうよ？　どうよ？ 今日のオレサマの証言ぶり！
English: Whaddya think of my performance today?

Japanese: シビれたっしょ？ 真宵ちゃん！
English: I had 'em swooning in the aisles! Huh, Maya?

Japanese: え‥‥あ、あたし？
English: S-swooning? Me?

Japanese: そ‥‥そうですね。 ビリッと来ちゃいました。
English: Oh... Oh, yes. I do remember feeling faint.

Japanese: だろ？　だろ？ もう、ホレちゃっただろ？
English: Right on! Tell me the truth, it was like love at first sight, right?

Japanese: なあ、成歩堂！
English: Right, Nick?

Japanese: え‥‥ぼ、ぼく？
English: H-huh? Me!?

Japanese: そ‥‥そうだな。 キュン、と来たかな‥‥。
English: I... uh, well, maybe my heart skipped a beat or two...

Japanese: なんか、イマイチ ピンと来ねぇなあ。
English: I think you can do better than that!

Japanese: 御剣が助かったのは、 オレのおかげよ？
English: C'mon! I saved Edgeworth in there, dude! Edgey!

Japanese: もっとこう、カンゲイしてくれよ！ このオレをよぉ！
English: You guys should be bowing before me! Yeah! Bow before your hero!

Japanese: なるほどくん！
English: Nick!

Japanese: かならず助けようね！ 御剣検事。
English: We have to save Mr. Edgeworth if it's the last thing we do, okay?

Japanese: ああ。
English: Right. (It very well may be...)

Japanese: とにかく、あのボート小屋の オジさん？
English: First, there's that rental boat shop caretaker.

Japanese: あの人のこと、調べようよ！
English: We need to find out who or what he is!

Japanese: そうだね。
English: I'd settle for who.

Japanese: （とりあえず、もう必要のない 証拠品を、少し整理するか‥‥）
English: (I guess I can clean out some of this evidence I no longer need...)

Japanese: よし、行こうか！
English: Okay, let's go!

Japanese: 千尋さんが“チャーリー”と 名づけてかわいがっていた植物。
English: Mia's favorite plant. Its name is "Charley."

Japanese: 最近は、真宵ちゃんも 世話に慣れてきたようで、
English: Maya's gotten the knack of watering it lately.

Japanese: チャーリーくん、元気を 取りもどしたみたいだ。
English: Charley's been perking up these days.

Japanese: ムズカシイ法律の本が あざ笑うように並んでいる。
English: Difficult-looking legal books stand in a formidable row. They mock me.

Japanese: ちょっと読んでみたら、 アタマが痛くなった。
English: I tried reading one, and it made my head hurt.

Japanese: しまうとき、落っことして 足まで痛くなった。
English: When I closed it, it slipped out of my hand. Then my foot hurt too.

Japanese: 今日も向かいのホテルは 大はんじょうだ。
English: Looks like it's cleaning day again at the hotel across the way.

Japanese: なんでも今度、支店を 出すそうだ。
English: I hear they're planning a second branch outside the city.

Japanese: ボーイが、引き出しのドライバーの 角度をなおしているのが見える。
English: I can see the bellboy, adjusting the angle of that screwdriver in the drawer.

Japanese: 千尋さんが使っていた デスクだ。
English: Mia's desk.

Japanese: お客がもっと増えれば 座る機会もあるだろうが、
English: If we had more clients, I would probably sit here more often.

Japanese: 今はソファでテレビを 見ていることの方が多い。
English: Lately, I've been spending more time on the couch, watching TV.

Japanese: 一度、真宵ちゃんがトノサマンの ポスターを持ってきて、
English: Maya brought in a poster of the Steel Samurai the other day.

Japanese: 張る、張らないで大ゲンカに なってしまった。
English: We had a big fight over whether to put it up or not.

Japanese: まだあきらめきれないらしく、 スキをうかがっているみたい。
English: I know she's just waiting for a chance to sneak it up on the wall.

Japanese: 矢張。 今日の法廷‥‥助かったよ。
English: Larry... You really helped out in the trial today.

Japanese: そうだよね。
English: You did!

Japanese: ヤッパリさんがいなかったら、 ユーザイだったもんね、御剣検事。
English: If you weren't there, Larry, I'm sure Mr. Edgeworth would have been found guilty!

Japanese: はっはっはっはっはっはっはっはっ はっはっはっはっはっはっはっはっ
English: Hah hah hah hah hah hah hah hah hah hah hah hah!

Japanese: でもよ、成歩堂。
English: But, seriously, Nick.

Japanese: たしかに、ボート小屋の管理人も アヤシイけどさあ。
English: That boat shop caretaker guy is pretty suspicious...

Japanese: 御剣のウタガイだって、まだ 晴れたワケじゃねえんだぜ。
English: But Edgey ain't off the hook yet!

Japanese: うう‥‥ん。 ヤッパリさん、シビアだなあ。
English: Way to spoil the mood, Larry.

Japanese: 客席で聞いているとよお。 こう言っちゃナンだけど‥‥、
English: Hey, I'm just a guy sitting in the audience, you know?

Japanese: 御剣だって、けっこう アヤシイ感じがするぜ‥‥。
English: But from where I was sitting, Edgey seemed pretty... edgy.

Japanese: おマエ、なんでそこまで アイツのこと、信じられるんだ？
English: I mean, can you really know he's telling the truth about that night?

Japanese: なるほどくん？
English: Nick?

Japanese: ぼくは‥‥。
English: I don't know.

Japanese: たとえ何があっても、 何が起こっても‥‥
English: But... what I do know is...

Japanese: お前たち２人のことだけは、 信じぬくつもりなんだよ。
English: I'm going to believe in you two until the end.

Japanese: ２人って？
English: Us two?

Japanese: 御剣検事と‥‥あたしでしょ？
English: Edgeworth and... who else? You mean me right?

Japanese: ナニ言ってんだよ！ オレだろ？　成歩堂！
English: Nah! He means me! Right, Nick?

Japanese: ああ。‥‥お前だよ、矢張。
English: Yeah, you, Larry.

Japanese: あたしじゃないんだ‥‥。
English: Not... me?

Japanese: でも。でも。 どうしてなの！　ヤッパリさん！
English: B-but why you, Larry!?
Huh?

Japanese: さ‥‥さあ。 どうしてだ？　成歩堂‥‥？
English: Um, actually, yeah. Why me, Nick?

Japanese: ‥‥いつもそこでダマる！
English: Hmph. Enough with the silent treatment!

Japanese: なるほどくん。
English: Nick...

Japanese: どうしてそんなに、 御剣検事にこだわるの？
English: Why do you trust Mr. Edgeworth so much?

Japanese: たしかに、最近はちょっと 感じが変わったけど、
English: I mean, he's changed recently, true.

Japanese: ただのイヤな男だったでしょ、 初めて会ったときは。
English: But when we first met him, he was kind of a jerk, don't ya think?

Japanese: 真宵ちゃんは、知らないんだよ。
English: You didn't know him back then.

Japanese: 弁護士を目指していたころの 御剣　怜侍を‥‥。
English: Back when he wanted to become a defense attorney.

Japanese: それって‥‥
English: Wait...

Japanese: なるほどくんたちがクラスメート だったっていう‥‥？
English: Was that when you two were classmates?

Japanese: うん。ぼくらが小学生のころの 話だ。
English: Yes. In grade school...

Japanese: ぼくは、助けられたんだ。 御剣　怜侍と‥‥
English: They saved me... Miles...

Japanese: 矢張　政志にさ。 ‥‥一生わすれないよ。
English: And Larry. They saved me and I'll never forget it.

Japanese: そのおかげで、こうして 弁護士にもなったんだ。
English: That's why I became a defense attorney, you know.

Japanese: ええええええええっ！
English: Whaaaat!?

Japanese: ね。ね。矢張さん。 なんのこと‥‥？
English: Hey, hey, Larry. What's he talking about?

Japanese: え！　ええと‥‥その。
English: Huh? Uh... umm...

Japanese: ‥‥悪ィ。 ゼンゼンおぼえてねえや、オレ。
English: Er, sorry. I kinda forgot.

Japanese: ううう。
English: Hmph.

Japanese: こら、なるほどくん！ 今日こそ聞くんだからね、その話！
English: Okay, Nick. Out with it! I'm going to hear this story today, and that's final!

Japanese: ‥‥わかったよ。
English: Okay, okay.

Japanese: 少し長くなるかもしれないけど、 聞いてもらおうかな‥‥。
English: It's kind of a long story, so hang in there.

Japanese: あれは、そう。 小学４年生の夏‥‥。
English: It was the beginning of Spring, 4th grade.

Japanese: ぼくは、学級裁判に かけられたんだ。
English: I was on trial. A class trial.

Japanese: が‥‥、がっきゅうさいばん？
English: A c-class trial!?

Japanese: 矢張、おぼえてないか？ 小学４年生の夏だ‥‥。
English: You remember, Larry? Spring, 4th grade?

Japanese: ぼくのクラスの１人の子が、 給食費を盗まれたんだ。
English: A kid in my class got his lunch money stolen.

Japanese: 給食費？
English: Lunch money?

Japanese: ぼくたちの小学校、 とても小さなところでさ。
English: Our school was really small.

Japanese: 毎月、親がお金を入れてくれた 封筒を、先生に提出するんだ。
English: Every month, kids would bring in an envelope with money for lunch from home.

Japanese: へええ‥‥。
English: Huh, I see.

Japanese: その封筒が‥‥消えたんだよ。 ３８００円、入っていた。
English: Anyway, this kid's envelope disappeared. With $38.00 still inside.

Japanese: ‥‥ああ。そんなことが あったっけなあ。
English: Oh... Yeah, now that you mention it, I do remember that!

Japanese: お前はよく知らないかも しれないな、矢張。
English: I can see why you'd forget, though.

Japanese: その日はお前、 学校を休んでいたから。
English: You were out of school that day.

Japanese: とにかく、封筒が盗まれたのは 体育の時間だった。
English: Anyway, the envelope had been stolen during PE class.

Japanese: ぼくはその日、カゼぎみで、 体育の授業を休んでいたんだ。
English: I was coming down with a cold, so I'd skipped PE that day.

Japanese: 授業を休んだのは、 ぼくだけだった。
English: I was the only one not in class.

Japanese: だから‥‥うたがわれたの？
English: So... they thought you did it?

Japanese: うん。クラス中のヤツが‥‥ ぼくをサイバンにかけろ、って。
English: Yeah. The kids in class said I should be put on trial.

Japanese: サイバン‥‥。
English: Trial...?

Japanese: 次の日、学級裁判が開かれた。 ‥‥もちろん、被告人はぼくだ。
English: So the next day we held a classroom trial, with me as the defendant.

Japanese: 「ぼ‥‥ぼくはやってないよ‥‥」
English: "I... I didn't do it!"

Japanese: 「有罪」「ゆうざい」「有罪」 「お前だろ！」「どろぼう！」
English: "Guilty!" "He did it!" "Guilty!" "It was you!" "Thief!"

Japanese: 「返してやれよ、お金」「ヒドい人 信じらんない！」「ゼッコーだ！」
English: "Give me my money back!" "You're such a meanie!" "No one play with him!"

Japanese: 「ぼくがやったって言えよ」「かく すんじゃねえよ」「言いなさいよ」
English: "Just admit you did it!" "You can't hide the truth!" "Tell us the truth!"

Japanese: 「遊んでやらないからな！」「もう ケシゴム貸してあげないからね！」
English: "We're not gonna play with you any more!" "Yeah, and no borrowing my eraser!"

Japanese: 「リレーの選手、やめさせようよ」 「図書委員もだ」「飼育係もよ！」
English: "He shouldn't be allowed in the relay race!" "Or on the library committee!"

Japanese: 「こないだの５０円返せ」「となり 町の銀行強盗、お前じゃねえのか」
English: "Gimme back my 50 cents I loaned you!" "Hey, did you rob that bank the other day?"

Japanese: 「龍一くん。ダメでしょ？ 人のお金を盗んじゃあ‥‥」
English: "Now, Phoenix, you know you shouldn't steal people's money! It's not right."

Japanese: ‥‥最後に、先生がそう言った。
English: In the end, even the teacher thought I'd done it.

Japanese: 「あやまりなさい。龍一くん」
English: "Apologize to the class, Phoenix."

Japanese: ‥‥その瞬間、ナニがなんだか わからなくなって‥‥
English: I... I didn't know what was happening.

Japanese: とても悲しくって‥‥ なみだが止まらなくって‥‥
English: I was so sad... I couldn't stop crying.

Japanese: クラス中の視線がぼくに集まった。 “オマエガハンニンダ”って‥‥。
English: Everyone was staring at me like I'd done it...

Japanese: ぼくは、あやまろうとした。 ‥‥その子の席の前まで行って。
English: I tried to apologize. I went over to where the boy was sitting...

Japanese: そのときだった。
English: That's when it happened.

Japanese: 「その必要はない！」
English: "He shouldn't have to apologize!"

Japanese: 「サイバンでモノを言うのは、 証拠品だけなのだ！」
English: "The only thing that belongs in a trial is evidence!"

Japanese: 「その他のものはすべて、 沈黙すべし！」
English: "Anything else has no place!"

Japanese: 「そんなコトも知らないのか‥‥ シロウトどもめッ！」
English: "You should all be ashamed... amateurs!"

Japanese: 「ミ‥‥ミツルギくん？」
English: "M-Miles?"

Japanese: 「キミじゃないのだろう？ ボクの封筒を盗んだのは‥‥」
English: "It wasn't you who stole my money, was it?"

Japanese: 「それならば、堂々としたまえ」
English: "Then you shouldn't apologize!"

Japanese: 「これだけ話し合ったのに、 キミがやったという証拠はない」
English: "Everyone's been shouting you did it, but no one has any proof!"

Japanese: 「ということは、裁判長！ ‥‥この少年は、ムザイだ！」
English: "That is why, Your Honor, this boy is innocent!"

Japanese: 「ナニを言ってるの、レイジくん！ 盗まれたのは、あなたのお金よ！」
English: "B-but Miles, it was your money that was stolen!"

Japanese: 「そうだよ！」 「そいつがやったんだよ！」
English: "Yeah yeah!" "He did it! He's the one!"

Japanese: 「証拠なんて必要ないわ」 「あやまらせろよ！」
English: "We don't need proof!" "Make him say he's sorry!"

Japanese: 「‥‥イイカゲンにしろよ！‥‥」
English: "Why don't you all just shut up!!!"

Japanese: 「おマエたち、いつもそうやって グルになって１人を責めて‥‥」
English: "This is always how it is, everybody ganging up and picking on one person."

Japanese: 「言われるヤツの身にも、 なってみろってんだよ！」
English: "Just think how he feels!"

Japanese: 「コイツ、“やってない”って 言ってるじゃねえかよォ！」
English: "He said he didn't do it, so he didn't do it!"

Japanese: 「わかりました」
English: "Very well."

Japanese: 「お金は、 先生が立て替えておきます」
English: "I will replace the money myself."

Japanese: 「‥‥学級裁判は、これで おしまいにしましょう‥‥」
English: "This class trial is over."

Japanese: それからだよ。
English: That's how it happened.

Japanese: ３人がなんとなく、 仲よくなったのは‥‥。
English: After that, the three of us were always friends.

Japanese: ‥‥そんなことが あったんだ‥‥。
English: Wow... I had no idea!

Japanese: あ、あったような気も するなあ‥‥。
English: Yeah... I had no idea, either! I mean, I forgot.

Japanese: あのとき、ぼくは 思い知らされたんだよ。
English: That's when I learned what it meant to be alone.

Japanese: 味方がいない、究極のコドク。 ‥‥その、ツラさをね。
English: Totally alone, without a friend in the world.

Japanese: ‥‥ヤッパリさん、 いいヒトだったんですね‥‥。
English: You did a good thing, Larry!

Japanese: い、いや、まあ‥‥。
English: Um, yeah, well...

Japanese: たまたまその日、学校 休んでたからよかったケド、
English: I was just lucky that I took the day off from school.

Japanese: そうじゃなかったらゼッタイ、 オレがうたがわれてたから。
English: If I'd been there they would have thought I'd done it!

Japanese: ヒトゴトじゃなかったんだわ、 コレが‥‥。
English: So, I took it kind of personally, see.

Japanese: （“事件のカゲに、ヤッパリ矢張” って言われつづけてたもんな）
English: ("When something smells, it's usually the Butz.")

Japanese: ‥‥学級裁判が終わって、 御剣から聞いたんだ。
English: Anyway, Edgeworth and I talked after that class trial.

Japanese: お父さんが弁護士だってこと。
English: That's when I heard his father was a defense attorney.

Japanese: 父親のコトを話すときだけは、 ヤツの目は子供っぽく光ってた。
English: I remember, his eyes would shine when he talked about his father.

Japanese: 「ボクはかならず、父さんのような 弁護士になるのだよ」
English: "I'm going to become a defense attorney, just like my Dad!"

Japanese: 「リッパな弁護士に‥‥」
English: "A famous defense attorney!"

Japanese: しかし‥‥それから数ヶ月後。 あいつは突然、転校していった。
English: Then, a few months later, he suddenly transferred to another school.

Japanese: ＤＬ６号事件‥‥。
English: The DL-6 Incident...

Japanese: そう。たぶん、お父さんが 亡くなったためだろう。
English: Right. I'm not sure, but the transfer probably had to do with his father's death.

Japanese: かわいそう‥‥。
English: That's so sad!

Japanese: 御剣の名前をひさしぶりに 見たのは、数年たってからだ。
English: It was several years later when I heard Edgeworth's name again.

Japanese: 新聞に、彼の名前が大きく のっていた。
English: There was an article about him in the newspaper.

Japanese: 《鬼検事に、黒い疑惑》 とかいう見出しでね。
English: The headline was something like "Dark Suspicions of a Demon Attorney."

Japanese: 証拠のでっち上げ、証言の操作、 もみ消し‥‥。
English: Fabricating evidence, manipulating testimonies, covering up facts...

Japanese: 有罪のためならば、なんでも する男だ、と書いてあった。
English: The article said he'd do anything to get a guilty verdict. Anything.

Japanese: ‥‥どうしてだ？
English: But why?

Japanese: ナニがあったんだよ！ まるで別人じゃねえか！
English: What happened!? I mean, that's not the Edgey I used to know at all!

Japanese: ぼくもそう思った。
English: That's what I thought too.

Japanese: ぼくは御剣に会おうと、 何度も連絡をとった。
English: I tried to get in touch with him I don't know how many times...

Japanese: しかし、彼は返事を してくれなかった。
English: He never replied.

Japanese: ムカシの友だちに‥‥ 会いたくなかったんだ‥‥。
English: I guess he didn't want to see his old friends...

Japanese: でもぼくは、 あきらめられなかった。
English: I couldn't just drop it, though.

Japanese: どうしても彼に会って、 ナゾを解くんだ、と‥‥
English: I wanted to meet him, to learn why he had become who he became.

Japanese: そう決心したんだ。
English: That's when I decided.

Japanese: ま‥‥まさか‥‥。
English: Wait... You don't mean...?

Japanese: まさか、なるほどくん‥‥ そのために‥‥？
English: That's why!?

Japanese: 御剣検事に会うために、 弁護士になったの‥‥？
English: That's why you became a defense attorney!? To meet Edgeworth!?

Japanese: 弁護士になれば、あいつは イヤでもぼくに会わざるをえない。
English: If I was a defense attorney, I knew he'd have to meet me whether he wanted to or not.

Japanese: 法廷でね。
English: In court.

Japanese: ぼくを信じてくれた御剣を、 ぼくは信じている。
English: Edgeworth believed in me, and I believe in him.

Japanese: あいつは苦しんでいる。 そして、味方がいない。
English: He's in pain... And no one's on his side.

Japanese: チカラになれるのは、本当の あいつを知っている、ぼくだけだ。
English: I'm the only one who knows the real Edgeworth. I'm the only one who can help him.

Japanese: な‥‥成歩堂‥‥。
English: Whoa... Nick.

Japanese: じゃあ、おマエがオレを、 タダで助けてくれたのも‥‥？
English: S-so, is that why you helped me out for free?

Japanese: ああ。
English: Uh... yes.

Japanese: お前を信じていたからだ。
English: I helped you because I believed in you.

Japanese: （‥‥ただし、タダと言った おぼえは、ないんだけど‥‥）
English: (Except I don't remember saying I'd do it for free...)

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥なるほどうよお‥‥‥‥
English: Aww, Nick! Nick!!!

Japanese: あたしに何か見せるより、 調査に行こうよ！
English: Stop showing me stuff and let's get cracking!

Japanese: もう、今日しかないんだよ！
English: We only have today to get to the bottom of this!

Japanese: いやあ、はっはっ。 カッコよかったよなー、オレ。
English: Woo hoo, I was hot out there. Hot!

Japanese: （ダメだ‥‥。 カンゼンに舞い上がってる）
English: (I'm glad someone's happy about how this case is going...)

Japanese: （今は、 何を見せてもムダだな‥‥）
English: (He seems too happy to care about anything I show him.)

Japanese: あと１日だね、なるほどくん。
English: One day left, Nick.

Japanese: ‥‥そうだな。
English: Yeah... I know.

Japanese: さあ。休んでるヒマはないよ！ 出かけようよ！
English: Well, no time to waste! Let's get going!

Japanese: どうしようか？ これから‥‥。
English: What do you think we should do now, Maya?

Japanese: それは、なるほどくんが イチバン、よく知ってるでしょ。
English: You would know best, Nick!

Japanese: そのとおりにがんばれば、 いいと思うよ。
English: Just do what you do! That should work.

Japanese: どう？ ‥‥何か気づいたこと、ある？
English: Well? Had any good ideas?

Japanese: ‥‥やっぱり、ＤＬ６号事件‥‥。
English: This is all tied to the DL-6 Incident...

Japanese: 明日までに、できるだけ 調べた方がよさそうだよね。
English: We'd better find out as much as we can about that murder before tomorrow!

Japanese: （御剣をがんじがらめにしている、 運命のクサリ‥‥か）
English: (Something that happened back then has a hold on Edgeworth and it won't let go.)

Japanese: １２月２７日　某時刻 留置所　面会室
English: December 27 Detention Center Visitor's Room

Japanese: 例によって、御剣は 取り調べを受けているらしい。
English: Looks like Edgeworth is back in questioning.

Japanese: 御剣検事には、聞かなきゃ いけないこと、あるよね。
English: We have our own questions for him!

Japanese: あとでまた来ようよ。
English: Let's come back later.

Japanese: そうだな‥‥。
English: Yeah, I guess so.

Japanese: わすれちゃダメだよ。
English: Don't forget, okay?

Japanese: 面会のようすを監視する看守だ。
English: This guard monitors the visitor's room.

Japanese: さっきから、まったく動かない。 さすが、プロだな。
English: He hasn't moved an inch since I came in. A real pro.

Japanese: ごていねいに、カメラで 見張っているようだ。
English: Smile for the camera...

Japanese: １２月２７日　某時刻 留置所　面会室
English: December 27 Detention Center Visitor's Room

Japanese: あいかわらず、シブいカオ してるなあ‥‥。
English: You look as grim as always.

Japanese: ‥‥フン。
English: Hmph.

Japanese: あの、御剣検事。聞きましたよ、 学級裁判の話。
English: Um, Mr. Edgeworth? I heard the story about the class trial...

Japanese: ‥‥学級裁判？　なんのことだ？
English: Class trial? What do you mean?

Japanese: え。おぼえてないんですか？
English: You... don't remember?

Japanese: ああ、おぼえていない。
English: No, I don't.

Japanese: 給食費を盗まれたんですよね？ 小学校４年生のとき‥‥。
English: Your lunch money was stolen, wasn't it? In 4th grade...?

Japanese: 給食費‥‥？
English: Lunch money...?

Japanese: あ、ああ。 そんなこともあったな‥‥。
English: Oh... Oh, right. Yes, I seem to remember something like that.

Japanese: なるほどくん。 ‥‥誰もおぼえてないよ。
English: Nick. I think you're the only one who really remembers.

Japanese: ま、まあ、ぼく以外の人間には どうでもいいことだろうからね。
English: Well, it probably only really mattered to me anyway.

Japanese: 御剣検事、知らないんですか？
English: Mr. Edgeworth, didn't you know?

Japanese: なるほどくん、あれがキッカケで 弁護士になったんですよ。
English: That trial was the reason Nick became a defense attorney!

Japanese: バカな。
English: Ridiculous!

Japanese: （‥‥ミもフタもない コメントだな‥‥）
English: (Gee... thanks.)

Japanese: ‥‥しかし。
English: That said...

Japanese: とてもキミらしい話だ。
English: It does sound like the kind of thing you'd do.

Japanese: ‥‥変わっていないな、成歩堂。 その、あきれるほどの単純さ‥‥。
English: You haven't changed a bit, have you, Wright? So...simple. To a fault, even.

Japanese: まあな。だが‥‥、
English: Well, maybe yeah, but...

Japanese: お前は変わっちまったな、御剣。
English: I think you changed too much, Edgeworth.

Japanese: そうだな。
English: Perhaps.

Japanese: ‥‥なんてこった‥‥。
English: This is bad...

Japanese: なるほどくん‥‥どうしよう。 とんでもないことに‥‥。
English: What are we going to do, Nick? What can we do!?

Japanese: くやしいけど‥‥
English: I don't know...

Japanese: ぼくたちじゃ、どうしようも ないよ‥‥。
English: I don't think there is anything we can do. Like it or not.

Japanese: ＤＬ６号事件の情報を、 詳しく知っている人がいれば‥‥。
English: If there's someone else who knows a lot about the DL-6 Incident, maybe...

Japanese: いるじゃない、なるほどくん！
English: There is, Nick!

Japanese: あの人に、助けてもらおうよ！
English: There is someone else who knows about DL-6!

Japanese: なあ、御剣。どうしてお前、 検事になったんだ？
English: Hey, Edgeworth. Why did you become a prosecutor, anyway?

Japanese: あんなに父親を尊敬して、 弁護士にあこがれていたのに‥‥。
English: You used to look up to your dad... You said you wanted to be a defense attorney, right?

Japanese: 私は、キミほどバカには なれなかったんだよ。
English: I couldn't let myself deny reality like you.

Japanese: ‥‥どういうことだ？
English: What do you mean?

Japanese: オヤジをうばわれ、それでも 犯罪者の味方になれるほど‥‥、
English: My father was taken from me, and you want me to defend criminals?

Japanese: 私はそこまで、お人よしではない！
English: I'm sorry, Wright, but I'm not that good of a person!

Japanese: お父さんが亡くなった事件‥‥ 容疑者が１人、逮捕されましたね。
English: One suspect was apprehended in your father's murder, right?

Japanese: ‥‥そうだ。父とエレベータに 閉じ込められた男‥‥。
English: Yes. The man trapped in the elevator with my father.

Japanese: 灰根　高太郎という名前だ。
English: His name... was Yanni Yogi.

Japanese: どう考えたって、犯人は あいつしかいないんだ！
English: He had to be the shooter, any way you look at it!

Japanese: なのに‥‥無罪になった。 あの弁護士のせいで！
English: Yet... he was found innocent. That defense attorney got him off the hook!

Japanese: （被害者の、 生倉弁護士のことか‥‥）
English: (That would be Robert Hammond...)

Japanese: ‥‥１５年前の、あの日。
English: On that day, 15 years ago...

Japanese: 我々３人は、５時間、 エレベータに閉じ込められた。
English: The three of us were trapped in that elevator for five hours.

Japanese: 助け出された時は 極度の酸欠状態になっていて、
English: When we were rescued, we all suffered oxygen deprivation.

Japanese: 私は、事件に関する記憶を なくしていたのだ。
English: I had lost all memory of the murder.

Japanese: 記憶を‥‥？
English: Lost your memory?

Japanese: 今も思い出せない。エレベータで、 何があったのか‥‥。
English: Even now, I can't recall what happened in that elevator.

Japanese: 法廷で、灰根の弁護士も そう主張した。
English: That was the crux of Yogi's attorney's argument in court.

Japanese: 酸欠で、灰根は 心神喪失状態にあった、と。
English: He claimed Yanni Yogi had been "not of sound mind" due to the oxygen deprivation.

Japanese: ‥‥結局、灰根は証拠不充分。 無罪になった。
English: Yogi was released due to a lack of evidence... innocent.

Japanese: それからだ。
English: That's when I changed my mind.

Japanese: 私が、弁護士というものに 憎しみを抱きはじめたのは‥‥。
English: I started to hate defense attorneys.

Japanese: 狩魔検事とは、どういう‥‥？
English: What's your relationship with von Karma?

Japanese: 彼は、私が最も尊敬する先輩だ。
English: He's my teacher, and a man who deserves respect.

Japanese: 私の法廷テクニックは、 彼にイチから仕込まれた。
English: I learned everything I know of courtroom techniques from him.

Japanese: なるほどくんで言えば、 お姉ちゃんみたいなもんだね。
English: So, he's like my sister was to you, Nick.

Japanese: 彼はすべてにおいて、 カンペキ主義者だ。
English: He is a perfectionist in all things.

Japanese: 法廷においても、 人生においても‥‥。
English: In court... In his personal life...

Japanese: カンペキであることに、 異常なほどのこだわりがあるのだ。
English: He is obsessed with doing everything perfectly.

Japanese: “カンペキ”‥‥？
English: Perfectly, huh?

Japanese: 彼があつかった事件で、未解決の まま時効になったものは、
English: In all the cases he has taken on, none were left unsolved.

Japanese: ただの１つもない。 ‥‥もちろん、敗訴も。
English: And not one suspect was declared innocent. Ever.

Japanese: でも‥‥それって‥‥。
English: But... but that's...

Japanese: わかっている。
English: I know.

Japanese: 中には、無実の人間が いたかもしれない。
English: It's possible some of the suspects were indeed innocent.

Japanese: しかし、私たちに、それを正確に 見抜くことは、不可能なのだ。
English: However, it is impossible for us to accurately determine that in every case.

Japanese: 狩魔検事は、彼のするべき仕事を カンペキに遂行しているだけだ。
English: All von Karma does is his job, to find the suspect guilty, perfectly.

Japanese: とにかく。
English: In any case...

Japanese: 彼の弱みを見つけるのは、 不可能に近い。
English: It's nigh well impossible to find a weakness in him.

Japanese: それをモミ消すためならば、彼は どんな手段でも使ってくるだろう。
English: Should a weakness appear, he would do everything in his power to make it go away.

Japanese: ‥‥あのなあ、御剣。
English: Um, Edgeworth?

Japanese: その話が本当なら、 お前も明日、有罪になっちゃうぞ。
English: If what you're saying is true, you're headed for a guilty sentence tomorrow!

Japanese: そ、そうですよ！ ホメてる場合じゃないでしょ！
English: H-he's right! Now's no time to praise the enemy, Mr. Edgeworth!

Japanese: ムム‥‥。
English: Mmph...

Japanese: それが‥‥ツラいところだ。
English: It's a strange situation in which I find myself, I'll admit.

Japanese: （やれやれ‥‥）
English: (No kidding.)

Japanese: 灰根　高太郎は、 法廷の係官だった。
English: Yanni Yogi was a court bailiff at the time.

Japanese: １５年前。私たちは偶然、 エレベータに乗り合わせた。
English: We just happened to be in that elevator together 15 years ago.

Japanese: ‥‥ヒドい揺れだった‥‥。
English: The quake was incredibly strong...

Japanese: 気がついたら‥‥ 我々は、闇の中にいた。
English: Before I knew it, everything was dark.

Japanese: 永遠とも思える、長い長い時間。
English: We were there for so long, it felt like forever.

Japanese: うすれてゆく空気‥‥。せまい 箱の中で、闇が迫ってくる‥‥。
English: The air thinned... and the darkness closed in on us in that little box.

Japanese: ‥‥しだいに我々は、落ちつきを うしなっていった。
English: We became... unsettled.

Japanese: 「助けてくれッ！　い、息が‥‥」
English: "H-help! I can't breathe!"

Japanese: 「うるさい！　ダマるんだ！ ‥‥こっちまでおかしくなる！」
English: "Quiet! I said quiet! You're not making this any easier!"

Japanese: 「出せ！　ここから 出してくれえッ！」
English: "I want to get out! Help! Get us out!"

Japanese: 「大声を出すな！　酸素をよけいに 消費するぞ！」
English: "Don't shout! You'll just use up more oxygen!"

Japanese: ‥‥私の記憶は、そこまでだ‥‥。
English: That's all I remember...

Japanese: 気がついたら、私は病院の ベッドで、天井をながめていた。
English: When I came to, I was in a hospital bed, staring up at the ceiling.

Japanese: 裁判では、灰根の精神状態が 問題になった。
English: In court, Yanni Yogi's mental condition was called into question.

Japanese: 酸欠と緊張状態が原因で、 灰根は心神喪失状態にあったとか。
English: They claimed the oxygen deprivation and stress had caused temporary insanity.

Japanese: 結局、その主張が認められて、 灰根は無罪になったのだ‥‥。
English: In the end, the claim passed the court, and Yogi was found innocent.

Japanese: ‥‥でも‥‥。 ちょっと、おかしいですね。
English: Huh... But, isn't that strange?

Japanese: この手紙‥‥。御剣検事に 復讐しろ、って書いてありますよ。
English: This letter tells him to "get revenge on Edgeworth"...

Japanese: なんで“復讐”なんですか？ それも、あなたに‥‥？
English: Why would he want to take revenge on you?

Japanese: 成歩堂。
English: Wright.

Japanese: ん？
English: Yeah?

Japanese: この数日間、ずっと悩んでいた ことがある。
English: There's something that's been troubling me these last few days.

Japanese: キミに話すべきかどうか‥‥。
English: I... didn't know whether or not I should tell you.

Japanese: もしかして、今日の 法廷で言っていた‥‥
English: You mean the nightmare?

Japanese: 私の‥‥悪夢の話だ。
English: It's... a nightmare I've had.

Japanese: 私が犯した罪の記憶‥‥。
English: A memory of a crime... that I committed.

Japanese: お前の‥‥罪？
English: A crime you committed?

Japanese: 殺人‥‥の記憶だ。
English: A memory... of a murder.

Japanese: ‥‥どうやら。
English: I think...

Japanese: キミに聞いてもらうときが 来たようだな‥‥。
English: I think the time has come to tell all.

Japanese: この１５年間、毎日のように 同じ夢を見る。
English: For the last 15 years, I've had the same dream almost every night.

Japanese: そのたびに私は、 恐怖にハネ起きるのだ。
English: I wake up in a fearful sweat, every time.

Japanese: ‥‥どんな夢なんですか‥‥？
English: What kind of dream?

Japanese: あの事件の夢だ。すべてが 闇に閉ざされた、あの事件。
English: It's a dream about my father's killing... in the dark.

Japanese: 「助けてくれッ！　い、息が‥‥」
English: "H-help! I can't breathe!"

Japanese: 「うるさい！　ダマるんだ！ ‥‥こっちまでおかしくなる！」
English: "Quiet! I said quiet! You're not making this any easier!"

Japanese: 「出せ！　ここから 出してくれえッ！」
English: "I want to get out! Help! Get us out!"

Japanese: 「大声を出すな！　酸素をよけいに 消費するぞ！」
English: "Don't shout! You'll just use up more oxygen!"

Japanese: 「い‥‥息ができねえ‥‥！ お前が空気をヨゴすからだ‥‥」
English: "I... I can't breath! You... you're using up my air!"

Japanese: 「な‥‥なんだと！」
English: "Wh-what!?"

Japanese: 「オレの空気を吸うな！ い‥‥息の根を止めてやる！」
English: "Stop breathing my air! I'll... I'll stop you!"

Japanese: 「う‥‥うわっ！ 何をする‥‥やめろ‥‥！」
English: "Aaaah! Wh-what!? What are you...!?"

Japanese: 「オレの空気を吸うなァァァァ！」
English: "Stop breathing my aaaaair!"

Japanese: （おとうさんが‥‥）
English: (No! Father!)

Japanese: （おとうさんが、 おそわれている‥‥！）
English: (He's attacking Father!)

Japanese: ‥‥ふと気がつくと、足元に ピストルがころがっている。
English: Then I see the pistol lying by my feet.

Japanese: その日の裁判の証拠品だったのか、 係官の持ちものだったのか‥‥。
English: I don't know if it was evidence from that day in court, or the bailiff's...

Japanese: 私は、夢中でそのピストルを 拾い上げる。
English: In a daze, I pick up the pistol...

Japanese: （ぼくのおとうさんから‥‥）
English: (Get away...!)

Japanese: （おとうさんから、 はなれろぉっ‥‥！）
English: (Get away from my father!)
*BANG*

Japanese: うぐおおおおおおおおおおおおお をををををををををををををッ！
English: Uuaaaaaaaaaaaaa aaaaaaaaaaaaargh!

Japanese: ‥‥その悲鳴で、目がさめるんだ。
English: And with that scream... I wake.

Japanese: ものすごい悲鳴だ。
English: It's a bone-chilling scream.

Japanese: この１５年間、耳からはなれたこと がない、ものすごい悲鳴‥‥。
English: A scream that has rung in my ears for the past 15 years.

Japanese: で、でも。
English: B-but...

Japanese: ‥‥それって、ただの“夢” なんですよね‥‥？
English: That's just a dream, right? Right?

Japanese: そう思って、この１５年間、 生きてきた。
English: That thought is the only thing that has kept me sane for the last 15 years.

Japanese: しかし‥‥。
English: But what if I'm wrong?

Japanese: これが“現実”だった としたら‥‥？
English: What if it's real?

Japanese: 人は、自分の精神を守るため、 記憶を閉ざすことがあるという。
English: They say that sometimes people shut out memories in self- defense.

Japanese: 父親の命をうばったのは、 他ならぬ、私かもしれない！
English: Maybe it was I who killed my father!

Japanese: そ‥‥そんなことって‥‥！
English: What!?

Japanese: ‥‥そう考えると、この手紙も なっとくできる。
English: If you think about it that way, this letter makes sense.

Japanese: 《御剣　怜侍に復讐しろ》
English: "Get your revenge on Miles Edgeworth."

Japanese: ‥‥そう。灰根は本当に無実 だったのだ‥‥。
English: Think about it. Yogi was really innocent.

Japanese: だから、復讐しようとしたのだ ‥‥この、私に。
English: That's why he wanted revenge against me!

Japanese: 待てよ、御剣！ ‥‥それって、つまり‥‥！
English: Wait, Edgeworth! You... you mean...!

Japanese: オヤジを撃ったのは‥‥ ＤＬ６号事件の真犯人は‥‥
English: It was me.

Japanese: この‥‥私‥‥ッ！
English: I was the true criminal of DL-6. I shot my father!

Japanese: 御剣。 ‥‥この手紙なんだけど‥‥。
English: Edgeworth... See this letter?
Hmm?

Japanese: 例の、ボート小屋の管理人の 金庫から出てきたんだ。
English: This came out of the safe in the shack where that boat rental caretaker lives.

Japanese: ほほう‥‥。
English: I see...

Japanese: この私に‥‥復讐だと‥‥？
English: Revenge... on me?

Japanese: いったい、誰なんですか！ あのオジさん‥‥！
English: Who is that old guy, anyway!?

Japanese: し‥‥知らん。
English: I... I don't know.

Japanese: お前がムカシ有罪にした、 無実の被告人、とか‥‥？
English: Could he be an innocent defendant you got declared guilty or something?

Japanese: イヤなことを言うな。 あんなオジさん、おぼえがないぞ。
English: Nice, Wright. But I don't remember that old man. Not at all.

Japanese: とにかく、オジさんは、 この手紙どおりに犯行を‥‥。
English: So, he was following this letter, then?

Japanese: つまり、この事件には 黒幕がいた、ということか‥‥。
English: Which means there was someone else behind it!

Japanese: 《お前の人生を破滅させた２人の 男に、今こそ復讐するのだ》
English: "Now is your time to get revenge on the two men who ruined your life!"

Japanese: ２人の男‥‥この私と、 生倉弁護士のこと‥‥か？
English: Two men... meaning myself and Robert Hammond?

Japanese: 《これが最後のチャンスだ》 とも書いてありますよ。
English: It also says: "This is your last chance!"

Japanese: 最後のチャンス‥‥？ もしかして、それって‥‥
English: Last chance...? Wait, maybe...

Japanese: “時効”のことじゃないか？
English: Maybe he's talking about the statute of limitations on the DL-6 Incident!

Japanese: まさか‥‥
English: Wait...

Japanese: まさか、あのオジさんが‥‥！
English: Wait, that old man...!

Japanese: ど、どうしたんですか！ 何かわかったの‥‥？
English: Wh-what is it!? Do you know who he is!?

Japanese: 灰根‥‥だったのか‥‥ あのオジさんが‥‥！
English: Yogi... Could he be Yogi!?

Japanese: はいね‥‥
English: Yogi?

Japanese: ＤＬ６号事件の容疑者で 無罪になった、あの男だ‥‥。
English: The suspect in the DL-6 Incident. The one who was found innocent.

Japanese: ‥‥今さら、こんな写真を 見せて、どうする気だ‥‥？
English: What are you showing me this picture for?

Japanese: い、いや、べつに。
English: Um, uh, no reason.

Japanese: しかし、今日の法廷での キミの推理、ミゴトだった。
English: You know, I was impressed by your deduction in the trial today.

Japanese: 苦しまぎれだったとはいえ、 なかなかだったぞ‥‥。
English: Granted, you were at the end of your rope, but still.

Japanese: なるほどくん、バレてるよ。 ‥‥苦しまぎれだったコト。
English: Nick, he noticed.

Japanese: ははは。
English: Hah hah.

Japanese: ‥‥私の人生を 変えてしまった事件だ。
English: It was that case that changed my life.

Japanese: １５年前の、１２月２８日‥‥。 明日で、時効か。
English: And tomorrow, on December 28, its statute of limitations runs out.

Japanese: （明日。‥‥偶然なのか‥‥？）
English: (Tomorrow... Could that be a coincidence?)

Japanese: だが‥‥。 書類上では時効になっても‥‥、
English: But... even if the case is finally closed on paper...

Japanese: 私の記憶から消すことは できない‥‥永久に‥‥。
English: It will never be erased from my memory. Never...

Japanese: 御剣検事‥‥。
English: Poor Mr. Edgeworth!

Japanese: ダメだよ、なるほどくん！
English: N-Nick! No!

Japanese: お‥‥お父さんの写真だよ。 そんなの見せたら‥‥。
English: Th-that's a photo of his father! Don't show him that!

Japanese: （‥‥そうだな）
English: (You're right...)

Japanese: （今さら見せるべきものじゃ ないか‥‥）
English: (Now probably isn't a good time to dredge up those memories...)

Japanese: ‥‥なんだ？
English: What is it?

Japanese: い、いや。‥‥なんでもない。
English: Uh, um, n-nothing.
Huh?

Japanese: すまないな。
English: Sorry.

Japanese: ‥‥何も言えることは ないようだ。
English: I'm not sure I can help you with that.

Japanese: １２月２７日　某時刻 ひょうたん湖公園　入口前
English: December 27 Gourd Lake Park Entrance

Japanese: おっ！　ひさしぶりッス！
English: Hey, pal! Long time no see!

Japanese: あ、イトノコ刑事さん。
English: Oh! Detective Gumshoe!

Japanese: 今日はアブなかったッス！
English: Close one today, eh!

Japanese: ハラハラして、ネクタイを１本 食いちぎったッス。
English: I got so worked up, I snapped my tie in half!

Japanese: そ、それはどうも スミマセンでした‥‥。
English: Uh... Sorry about that.

Japanese: いいッス！　おかげで、 ハンニンがわかったッスからな！
English: No prob, pal! Thanks to you, we now know who really did it!

Japanese: ボート小屋のオジさんですか？
English: You mean, the boat shop caretaker?

Japanese: 約束するッス！
English: Look, I'll make you a promise.

Japanese: 自分は、明日の開廷時間までに、 必ずアイツをタイホするッス！
English: I'll have that scoundrel in my custody by trial time tomorrow!

Japanese: ナニがあっても！
English: Come what may!

Japanese: ‥‥それが、アンタに対する 警察官としてのレイギッス！
English: It's my duty to you as a police officer!

Japanese: では、シツレイするッス！
English: Now, I'm off to catch me a criminal!

Japanese: イトノコ刑事、ハリきってたねー。
English: Detective Gumshoe sure is... active today.

Japanese: あ、それから１つ。
English: Oh, one other thing!

Japanese: きゃっ！
English: Eek!

Japanese: 今日は、森の方へは 行けないッス。
English: No one can go into the woods today.

Japanese: もり？
English: The woods?

Japanese: （ナツミさんがキャンプしていた ところか‥‥）
English: (Where Lotta was camping?)

Japanese: あそこはキャンプ禁止だったッス。 公園の管理人にバレて、
English: The woods are off-limits to camping, and apparently the park ranger found out.

Japanese: しばらく、立入禁止になったッス。
English: He got pretty mad. No one can go in for a while.

Japanese: ナツミさん、怒られちゃったんだ。
English: I guess Lotta's in a 'lotta' trouble...

Japanese: ま、そういうことッス。 ‥‥じゃ、また明日、会うッス！
English: Anyway, I'll be seeing you tomorrow!

Japanese: １２月２７日　某時刻 ひょうたん湖広場
English: December 27 Gourd Lake Public Beach

Japanese: あれ。あの目ざわりな トノサマンがいないな‥‥。
English: Huh? The Steel Eyesore is missing...

Japanese: “目ざわり”って言うな！
English: "Eyesore"!?

Japanese: 屋台も休みみたいだ。
English: Looks like the hotdog stand is closed, too.

Japanese: たしかに、アルバイト どころじゃないのかもね‥‥。
English: I guess Larry's been too busy with the trial to show up for work...

Japanese: １２月２７日　某時刻 貸しボート小屋前
English: December 27 Boat Rental Shop

Japanese: あのオジさん、逃げちゃった んだよね。
English: That old caretaker got away...

Japanese: ああ。
English: Yep.

Japanese: まさか、あのオジさんが ハンニンだったなんて‥‥。
English: I never imagined he might be the real murderer!

Japanese: ウォッホォン！
English: *Ah-HHHHEM!*

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥！ （このセキ払いは‥‥）
English: (I'd know that clearing- of-the-throat anywhere!)

Japanese: やあやあチミたち。 ‥‥どうした？　こんなところで。
English: Ah hah, hello! What might you be doing here?

Japanese: チミたちもサンポかな？　ん？
English: Out for a walk, hmm?

Japanese: “青春の日々はぁ‥‥青いレモンの カオリぃ‥‥”とォ。
English: "Ahh... the days of my youth... like the scent of fresh lemon..." you see.

Japanese: ほ‥‥星影センセイ！
English: Mr. Grossberg!

Japanese: なにをノンキなコト 言ってるんですか！
English: This is no time for idle reminiscing!

Japanese: 御剣検事の裁判、明日が 最終日なんですよ！
English: Mr. Edgeworth's trial ends tomorrow!

Japanese: ‥‥そ、それはそうぢゃが。
English: Er... that is true, yes.

Japanese: 今日の法廷を見たかぎりでは、 もうあの子はアンシンぢゃろ？
English: But, from what I saw of today's trial, Edgeworth should be fine, right?

Japanese: それが‥‥ そうでもないんです。
English: Well... I'm not so sure about that.

Japanese: ‥‥どういうことぢゃ？
English: Ho ho! What do you mean by that?

Japanese: それが、まだ‥‥ ハッキリしないんですけど。
English: Well... I'm not sure.
Hmm...

Japanese: 何かわかったら、 ワシの事務所に来なさい。
English: If you find anything out, come by my office at once.

Japanese: チカラになれることが あるかもしれん。
English: I may be able to offer you some assistance.

Japanese: （センセイ、行っちまったぞ‥‥）
English: Thanks... (Bye...)

Japanese: 星影センセイ、何してたのかな？ ‥‥こんなところで‥‥。
English: What do you think Mr. Grossberg was doing here anyway?
Who knows...

Japanese: １２月２７日　某時刻 警察署内　刑事課
English: December 27 Police Department Criminal Affairs

Japanese: （イトノコさん‥‥ まだ、もどってないみたいだな）
English: (Hmm... Looks like Detective Gumshoe hasn't gotten back yet.)

Japanese: イトノコなら、今日は もどらないと思うよ。
English: Gumshoe? He won't be coming back today.

Japanese: あ‥‥そうなんですか。
English: Oh... really?

Japanese: 明日までに、どうしても逮捕 しなくちゃならん男がいるとか。
English: He said there was some guy he had to arrest by tomorrow.

Japanese: （管理小屋のオジさんか‥‥）
English: (The boat shop caretaker...)

Japanese: 命にかえても見つけだすッス！ ‥‥って叫んで、出ていったよ。
English: He shouted something about "catching him if it's the last thing I do, pal!"

Japanese: （ありがとう‥‥イトノコさん）
English: (Good luck, Gumshoe!)

Japanese: １２月２７日　某時刻 警察署内　刑事課
English: December 27 Police Department Criminal Affairs

Japanese: ‥‥人が少ないね。
English: There's hardly anyone here...

Japanese: みんな、オジさん‥‥灰根を 探しに行ってるんだよ。
English: Everyone must be out looking for the old guy... Yogi.

Japanese: あ。キミたち。
English: Ah, it's you.

Japanese: 今日は、イトノコはもう もどらないよ。
English: I don't think Gumshoe'll be coming back today.

Japanese: 徹夜でヒト探しだとさ。
English: He's staying out late looking for someone.

Japanese: イトノコ刑事さんも がんばってくれてるんだね！
English: Sounds like Detective Gumshoe is pounding the pavement for real!

Japanese: あの。あたしたち、資料室に 行きたいんですけど‥‥。
English: Um, we were wondering if we could check out the Records Room again...

Japanese: ‥‥勝手に入ってもらっちゃ こまるんだけど‥‥。
English: Well, now, I can't have just anyone wandering around in there.

Japanese: まあ、今なら狩魔検事が いると思うから、
English: But... I guess Mr. von Karma is in there now, anyway.

Japanese: 彼といっしょなら、資料を 見てもいいよ。
English: You can go in as long as he's there.

Japanese: カルマ検事‥‥？
English: von Karma...?

Japanese: ああ。 ‥‥ついさっき、来たところだ。
English: Yes. He just arrived actually.

Japanese: （狩魔検事が、資料室に！）
English: (von Karma's in the Records Room!)

Japanese: い、急ごうよ、なるほどくん！
English: N-Nick! Let's hurry!

Japanese: １２月２７日　某時刻 星影法律事務所
English: December 27 Grossberg Law Offices

Japanese: ‥‥また、いない。
English: He's out... again.

Japanese: あのセンセイ、いつ シゴトしてるの？
English: When does he work, anyway?

Japanese: まあまあ。 （また、あとで来てみるか‥‥）
English: Now, now. Don't be harsh. (Guess we'll have to come back later.)

Japanese: 星影センセイの絵、 どうやら帰ってこないみたいだな。
English: I guess Mr. Grossberg isn't getting that painting back.

Japanese: ちょっとかわいそうな 気もする‥‥。
English: I feel kind of bad for him...

Japanese: マホガニー製のどっしりした デスクだ。
English: A solid mahogany desk.

Japanese: 深いツヤが出るまで みがき込まれている。
English: The wood's been polished to a deep luster.

Japanese: 来客用のテーブルだ。
English: A table for clients.

Japanese: 上品な黒檀のシガレットケースと、 純金製のライター、か？
English: Hmm... an elegant ebony case, and if I'm not mistaken, that lighter's made of solid gold.

Japanese: なんだかわからないけど、 高そうなことに間違いはない。
English: Even I can tell someone here's got money to burn.

Japanese: 最高級の観葉植物だ。
English: An expensive potted plant.

Japanese: 名前はわからないが、 どうせ高いんだろう。
English: No idea what kind of plant it is, but it's probably the most expensive one available.

Japanese: マホガニー製の高そうな本棚に、 高そうな本がズラリ。
English: Expensive-looking mahogany bookshelves, filled with expensive-looking books.

Japanese: ‥‥でも、あまり読まれたような あとがないのが気になるな‥‥。
English: Hmm... funny... They don't look like they've ever been read.

Japanese: １２月２７日　某時刻 星影法律事務所
English: December 27 Grossberg Law Offices

Japanese: ほ‥‥星影センセイ！
English: M-Mr. Grossberg!

Japanese: お。チミたち。‥‥なんぢゃ、 シンコクそうなカオして。
English: Ah, hello there. What's wrong? You look troubled.

Japanese: センセイこそなんですか！ ノンキなカオして！
English: No kidding! I can't believe you're not!

Japanese: ‥‥い、いやいやいや。
English: M-my my my...

Japanese: いったい、ナニがあったんぢゃ。 ‥‥話してみなさい。
English: Just calm down and tell me what's happened, hmm?

Japanese: み、御剣検事が‥‥ みつるぎけんじがああ！
English: It's M-Mr. Edgeworth! He... He... *sob*

Japanese: ‥‥なんと‥‥
English: I see...

Japanese: 御剣　信を撃った夢を‥‥のう。
English: So, Edgeworth dreamt he shot his own father?

Japanese: ゆ、“夢”なんですからね！ ‥‥あくまで！
English: It's o-only a dream! Only a dream!

Japanese: それはどうかのお‥‥。
English: I wonder...
W-what...?

Japanese: それならばなぜ、チミたちは そんなにあわてておるのかね？
English: If that's the case, then why do you two look so troubled, hmm?

Japanese: そ、それは‥‥。
English: W-well...

Japanese: それに。
English: Also, consider this.

Japanese: あきらかに灰根は、御剣　怜侍に 対して、悪意を持っておる。
English: Yogi quite certainly holds a deep grudge against Miles Edgeworth.

Japanese: 殺人罪を着せようとするほどの 強く、深い悪意を‥‥。
English: So deep, he'd want to frame him for murder.

Japanese: ‥‥おそらく。
English: This leads me to surmise...

Japanese: 夢ではあるまい。 ‥‥現実だったのぢゃよ。
English: That Mr. Edgeworth's dream was NOT a dream. It was real.

Japanese: チミたちの想像どおり、な。
English: As you imagined.

Japanese: 御剣　怜侍は、父親を助けるために ピストルを投げつけ‥‥
English: Miles Edgeworth threw the pistol to save his father...

Japanese: そのピストルが‥‥ 暴発したんぢゃ。
English: the pistol fired... and the deed was done.

Japanese: そ‥‥そんな‥‥。
English: N... No!

Japanese: そんなの、ヒドすぎるよ‥‥。
English: I don't believe it!

Japanese: 灰根は、その罪を着せられて、 係官としての人生をつぶされた。
English: Yogi was suspected of murder, and his career as a bailiff was irrevocably wrecked.

Japanese: だから‥‥御剣　怜侍に 復讐しようとしたのだ。
English: Thus, he sought revenge on Miles Edgeworth.

Japanese: 時効直前の‥‥今になって、な。
English: This was his last chance, of course, with the statute of limitations so close.

Japanese: なるほどくん‥‥どうしよう。
English: What do we do, Nick?

Japanese: カルマ検事、きっとＤＬ６号事件の こと、持ち出してくるよ。
English: von Karma is going to bring up DL-6, you can bet on it!

Japanese: 御剣検事‥‥もしかしたら‥‥ 罪を、認めちゃうかも‥‥。
English: What if Mr. Edgeworth pleads guilty to DL-6!?

Japanese: そんなことはさせない！
English: I won't let him!

Japanese: ‥‥ぢゃが、成歩堂くん。
English: Erm, yes, Mr. Wright...

Japanese: ザンコクな話かもしれんが‥‥、
English: I hate to say this...

Japanese: たとえ過失でも、殺人は 殺人ぢゃよ‥‥？
English: But even accidental murder is murder, you know.

Japanese: わかってますよ！
English: I know that!

Japanese: ただ、ぼくはまだ、 信じているんです！　御剣を。
English: I... I just believe in Edgeworth's innocence!

Japanese: あいつが、殺人なんて‥‥！
English: I can't believe he'd kill someone!

Japanese: で‥‥でも！
English: B-but, Nick!

Japanese: 御剣検事、自分で 認めちゃっているんだよ？
English: Mr. Edgeworth admits it himself!

Japanese: それに、お父さんも、御剣検事を 守るために、ウソを‥‥
English: His father must have lied to protect him, from beyond the grave...

Japanese: ‥‥それでも！
English: I don't care!

Japanese: それでも、あいつは無実なんだ！
English: I know he's not guilty!

Japanese: 成歩堂くん。
English: Mr. Wright...

Japanese: チミがそこまで言うなら‥‥、 もう一度、調べてみるんぢゃ。
English: If you say so, I suppose I could go check again.

Japanese: 警察の‥‥資料をな。
English: The police files might hold something of interest.

Japanese: 星影センセイ‥‥。
English: Mr. Grossberg... thank you!

Japanese: ほとんど可能性はゼロぢゃが。 ‥‥何か、見つかるかもしれん。
English: I can't promise anything. In fact, I think the chances of finding something are slim.

Japanese: わかりました。
English: I understand.

Japanese: （警察の‥‥資料か‥‥）
English: (The police materials... hmm.)

Japanese: 御剣の父親って、どういう‥‥？
English: What do you know about Edgeworth's father?

Japanese: すばらしい弁護士ぢゃった。
English: He was a defense attorney without peer.

Japanese: 月並みな表現ぢゃが、 そうとしか言いようがない。
English: It sounds trite, but it's true.

Japanese: ‥‥そういえば、似ておったかも しれんなあ。
English: Well, he may have had one peer now that I think about it.

Japanese: 綾里　千尋クンと‥‥。
English: Your mentor, Mia Fey.

Japanese: お姉ちゃんと‥‥？
English: My sister...?

Japanese: 御剣　信は、狩魔検事のやり方に、 強く反発を感じていた。
English: Gregory Edgeworth was very disapproving of Mr. von Karma's techniques.

Japanese: （カルマケンジ‥‥）
English: (That's no surprise...)

Japanese: 狩魔は強引な男ぢゃ。証拠・証言の でっち上げなど、平気でする‥‥。
English: von Karma is an extreme man. Forged testimonies and evidence are nothing to him.

Japanese: 当然、彼のあつかう事件は、 すべて勝訴だ。
English: The result: he has a perfect win record in court.

Japanese: 御剣　信は、狩魔に勝つことで、 ヤツのやり方を否定しようとした。
English: To beat him, Gregory Edgeworth tried to call attention to his methods.

Japanese: そ‥‥それで？
English: And...?

Japanese: 負けたよ。
English: He lost.

Japanese: そして、死んだのぢゃ。 ‥‥失意のまま、な。
English: And died in despair, as it were.

Japanese: そうなんですか‥‥。
English: I see...

Japanese: 御剣　信が殺害されて、 １人の霊媒師が呼ばれた。
English: When Gregory Edgeworth was killed, the police called on a spirit medium.

Japanese: あんたのお母さん‥‥ 綾里　舞子ぢゃよ。
English: That was your mother, Misty Fey.

Japanese: 『私、御剣　信を殺したのは‥‥』
English: "I am Gregory Edgeworth... I have been killed..."

Japanese: 『私を撃ったのは、 法廷係官の、灰根　高太郎‥‥』
English: "The one who shot me... was the bailiff... Yanni Yogi."

Japanese: ‥‥しかし、灰根は無実ぢゃった。
English: Yet Yogi was found innocent.

Japanese: おかあさん、そのせいで‥‥ 出て行っちゃったんだ‥‥。
English: That's when my mother left us.

Japanese: “インチキだ”って、 みんなから言われて‥‥。
English: Everyone called her a fraud.

Japanese: そう。みんな、そう思った。 綾里　舞子はインチキだ、と。
English: That's right. Everyone thought she was, you see.

Japanese: ‥‥ぢゃが‥‥今、思えば‥‥
English: Yet... now that I think about it.

Japanese: ウソをついたのは、御剣　信の霊 だったのではないだろうか？
English: It seems the one who lied was Gregory Edgeworth's ghost...

Japanese: 御剣　信は、知っていたのぢゃ。 本当は、誰が自分を撃ったのかを。
English: Gregory Edgeworth must have known who shot him.

Japanese: ‥‥まさか‥‥
English: I don't believe it...

Japanese: 御剣　怜侍を‥‥ 自分の息子を守るために、
English: So you're saying he falsified his "testimony"?

Japanese: 偽証したって言うんですか？ 御剣のオヤジが‥‥！
English: That Edgeworth's dad lied to protect his son...?

Japanese: ‥‥可能性の話ぢゃ‥‥。
English: It's only a possibility, mind you.

Japanese: しかし。否定はできん。
English: But... a possibility nonetheless.

Japanese: この手紙を書いたのが狩魔ならば、 ヤツは当然、知っておるはずぢゃ。
English: If it truly was von Karma who wrote this letter, then he would know the truth.

Japanese: 御剣　怜侍が、あやまって父親を 殺害してしまったということを。
English: He would know that Miles Edgeworth had accidentally killed his own father.

Japanese: おそらく明日の法廷で、狩魔は そのことを持ち出すぢゃろう。
English: He'll say as much tomorrow in court, I should think.

Japanese: 御剣　怜侍に“有罪”を 認めさせるために‥‥な。
English: He'll press the point until the court finds Miles Edgeworth "guilty."

Japanese: そ‥‥そんな‥‥。
English: Oh no!

Japanese: でも、でも。カルマ検事がなぜ、 御剣検事のことを知ってるの！
English: B-but how could von Karma know about Mr. Edgeworth's past like that?

Japanese: 御剣検事自身、“悪夢”だって 思っていたのに‥‥。
English: Even Mr. Edgeworth thought it was just a nightmare!

Japanese: ‥‥たしかに‥‥ それは、わからない。
English: Hmm... That, I do not know.

Japanese: しかし。ヤツはカンペキで、 ‥‥そして、執念ぶかい。
English: Yet I do know that von Karma is both persistent... and a perfectionist.

Japanese: 御剣　信に対する憎しみを、息子に ぶつけるつもりかもしれん‥‥。
English: He may be seeking to satisfy a grudge against Gregory Edgeworth by hurting his son.

Japanese: ‥‥どういうことですか‥‥？
English: What do you mean?
It was fifteen years ago...

Japanese: 狩魔は、たしかに 弁護士・御剣　信に勝利した。
English: von Karma met Gregory Edgeworth in court, and von Karma did win...

Japanese: しかし‥‥、狩魔も、 無傷ではすまなかったのぢゃ。
English: But he didn't make it through the trial unscarred.

Japanese: 御剣のオヤジと狩魔の 勝負って‥‥どうだったんですか？
English: What happened in the trial between Edgeworth's dad and von Karma?

Japanese: 判決は“有罪”‥‥ 狩魔の勝利ぢゃった。
English: von Karma got the "guilty" verdict he wanted. He won the trial.

Japanese: しかし、御剣　信の告発によって、 狩魔の提出した証拠に‥‥、
English: But Gregory Edgeworth accused von Karma of faulty evidence.

Japanese: 一部、不正なものがあったことが 立証されたのぢゃ。
English: And though he lost the trial, Mr. Edgeworth's accusation stood.

Japanese: 不正な証拠‥‥。
English: Faulty evidence?

Japanese: 狩魔は、検事生活でただ一度の 処罰を受けた‥‥。
English: It was the only penalty von Karma has ever received in his career as a prosecutor.

Japanese: 御剣　信は、狩魔のカンペキな 経歴に、キズをつけたのぢゃよ。
English: Gregory Edgeworth dealt a blow to his perfect trial record.

Japanese: へええ‥‥。
English: Wow.

Japanese: そのコトが、よほどショック だったのぢゃろう‥‥
English: It must have been quite a shock for von Karma.

Japanese: 狩魔検事は、その日から数ヶ月、 休暇をとったほどぢゃ。
English: He took a vacation for several months after that, you see.

Japanese: 休暇を‥‥？
English: A vacation...?

Japanese: 長い検事生活で、狩魔が “休暇”をとったのは、
English: Yes, an unusual event for the man.

Japanese: それが最初で‥‥そして、 最後ぢゃったよ。
English: That was the first, and the last vacation he's taken in his many years of prosecuting.

Japanese: えー！ お休みしないんですか？
English: Really!? He doesn't take vacations!?

Japanese: 海とか、山とか‥‥ それに、ヨーロッパとか！
English: Like... go to the sea, or, uh, to the mountains?
Don't tell me he's never been to Europe!?

Japanese: なんだよ、そのよくわからない 休暇のイメージは。
English: You have strange ideas about vacations, Maya.

Japanese: ‥‥とにかく。
English: In any case...

Japanese: 狩魔が休暇をとったのは、 そのときだけぢゃ。
English: That was the only time he took a vacation from work.

Japanese: それだけ、くやしかったの ぢゃろう‥‥。
English: I believe the penalty upset him quite a lot.
(Odd... If he wanted to keep a perfect record so badly...)

Japanese: （カンペキな経歴を残したいなら、 それで休むかな‥‥？）
English: (why would he take such a long vacation?)

Japanese: ‥‥ほほお。 それが、問題の手紙か‥‥。
English: Oh ho! So this is the letter?

Japanese: たしかに、 灰根はこのとおりに動いて、
English: It does seem that Yogi was following this letter...

Japanese: 生倉を殺害したんぢゃろう。
English: when he killed Hammond.

Japanese: でも‥‥なぜ生倉弁護士を‥‥？
English: But, why kill Robert Hammond?

Japanese: ‥‥生倉は ウデの立つ弁護士ぢゃったが‥‥
English: Hammond was a skilled defense attorney.

Japanese: ヤツは依頼人のためではなく、 自分のために弁護をしておった。
English: But... he defended clients not for their sake, but for his own.

Japanese: “自分のため”‥‥？
English: Huh? His own sake?

Japanese: ヤツは、自分の依頼人を 信じようとはしなかった。
English: He never trusted his clients, that one.

Japanese: 信じていたのは、 自分のウデのみぢゃ。
English: The only thing he trusted was his own ability.

Japanese: でも、結果がムザイなら、 同じなんじゃあ‥‥？
English: But, he got his client found innocent. So why should it matter?

Japanese: 違うんぢゃよ。 ‥‥それはもう、全然な。
English: Actually, my dear, it's quite different.

Japanese: 生倉は、自分のために ムザイ判決を勝ちとった。
English: He won that innocent verdict for no one but himself.

Japanese: その結果、灰根は社会的に 抹殺されてしまったのぢゃ。
English: Yogi was a free man, but socially, he was ruined.

Japanese: （どういうことだ‥‥？）
‥‥チミにも、 今にわかるぢゃろう。
English: You'll understand soon enough.

Japanese: はて。
English: Wait!

Japanese: どうしました？
English: What is it?

Japanese: この手紙‥‥ この、文字じゃが。
English: This letter...

Japanese: 見たことがある。この筆跡。 ‥‥とおいムカシ、どこかで。
English: I've seen this handwriting somewhere before... a long time ago!

Japanese: いったい、 誰の字ぢゃったかな‥‥。
English: Whose handwriting was this...?

Japanese: チミ、心当りはないかね。 この手紙、誰が書いたか‥‥？
English: Do you have any idea who wrote this?

Japanese: 御剣　怜侍ですか？
English: Miles Edgeworth?

Japanese: な、な、なんと！
English: Wh-wh-whaaat!?

Japanese: 彼がなんのため、こんなものを 書くのぢゃ！
English: Why in heavens would he write something like this!?

Japanese: この手紙は、御剣　怜侍を おとしいれるためのモノぢゃろが！
English: Why, this letter is an attempt to destroy Miles!

Japanese: もっと、よく考えて しゃべるんぢゃ！　ヒヨっ子め！
English: Think before you speak, greenhorn!

Japanese: （大目玉をくらってしまった‥‥）
English: (Uh oh. He's angry.)

Japanese: もう一度、聞くぞ。
English: Think again!

Japanese: 灰根　高太郎ではないでしょうか？
English: Maybe it was Yanni Yogi?

Japanese: 灰根　高太郎ぢゃと？
English: Yanni Yogi!?

Japanese: ‥‥彼が、自分自身に手紙を書き、 自分でそれを実行したと？
English: You claim he wrote himself this letter... then followed his own instructions?

Japanese: え‥‥そ、そうなりますね。
English: Uh... Yeah, I guess that would be what happened.

Japanese: なんぢゃ、灰根という男は ２重人格者なのかの？
English: Harumph! Perhaps you think Mr. Yogi has a split- personality, hmm?

Japanese: ‥‥可能性は、 あると思いますけど‥‥。
English: I think that's definitely a possibility, yes...
Hmm...

Japanese: いや。違うの。ワシは、灰根という 男のことを知らん。
English: No, I think not. I do not know this Yogi, in any case.

Japanese: だから、ヤツの字に見おぼえが あるワケがない。
English: There's no way I would recognize his handwriting.

Japanese: はあ、なるほど‥‥。
English: Oh, right...

Japanese: はあ、ぢゃない。 ‥‥もう一度、聞くぞ。
English: Yes, Wright. I'll ask you again.

Japanese: もしかして、狩魔　豪ですか？
English: Hmm... could it be Manfred von Karma?

Japanese: カルマ？　なんでここに、 ヤツの名前が出てくるんぢゃ？
English: von Karma? Why would he have something to do with this?

Japanese: いや、なんで、と言われても‥‥。
English: Um, well, I'm not sure...

Japanese: ん？ ‥‥カルマ‥‥。
English: Hmm? von Karma... von Karma...

Japanese: そ、そうぢゃ。
English: W-wait! You're right, my boy!

Japanese: たしかに‥‥これは、 狩魔検事の字ぢゃ！
English: This is von Karma's handwriting, I'm sure of it!

Japanese: ムカシ、法廷の書類でよく見た‥‥ 彼の字ぢゃよ！
English: I used to see it all the time on court reports...

Japanese: ええええっ！ ‥‥じゃ、じゃあ‥‥！
English: Whaaaaaat!? But... but that means...!

Japanese: 灰根さんに、殺人の指示を 送ったのは‥‥
English: Th-the one who told Mr. Yogi to kill was...

Japanese: ‥‥そう。 狩魔　豪‥‥ということになる。
English: Correct. Manfred von Karma, himself!

Japanese: ど‥‥どういうことだ‥‥！
English: What does this mean, then?

Japanese: なぜ‥‥狩魔検事が、御剣を‥‥？
English: Why would von Karma want to frame Edgeworth...?

Japanese: 間違いない。この手紙は、 狩魔が書いたものぢゃ。
English: No doubt about it, this is von Karma's handwriting.

Japanese: 灰根はこのとおりに動いて、 生倉を殺害したんぢゃろう。
English: Yogi was following von Karma's instructions when he killed Hammond.

Japanese: 問題は‥‥。
English: Now, there's only one question left.

Japanese: なぜ、狩魔が今になって、
English: Why did von Karma write this?

Japanese: こんな手紙を書いたか、 ぢゃな‥‥。
English: And why now?

Japanese: とりあえず、撃っているのが 御剣クンでなくて、何よりぢゃ。
English: In any case, it's good that the one shooting isn't Miles.

Japanese: あたりまえですよ！
English: You bet it's good!

Japanese: （犯人のヤツ‥‥）
English: (I can't believe the fiendish planning that went into this murder...)

Japanese: （こんな手のこんだ ワナを考えるなんて‥‥）
English: (And we almost fell right into his trap!)

Japanese: ヒドいヤツだよね！
English: What a creep!

Japanese: 美しいヒトぢゃったよ。
English: Ah, she was a beautiful woman...

Japanese: ‥‥まこと、スマナイことを したもんぢゃ‥‥。
English: I'm truly sorry about what I did...
Huh?

Japanese: どうしてセンセイが あやまるんですか‥‥？
English: Sorry about what?

Japanese: （ぼくが口を挟むところじゃ ないな‥‥）
English: (I think I'll stay out of this one...)

Japanese: この事件が起こったのが、 １５年前の明日‥‥。
English: This incident took place 15 years ago tomorrow.

Japanese: 明日が終わったとき、 この事件も終わるんぢゃよ。
English: So tomorrow will see the completion of not one, but two trials.

Japanese: “時効”という ベンリなコトバによってな。
English: All thanks to the statute of limitations.

Japanese: ‥‥しかし。
English: However...

Japanese: 事件は終わることなどない。 ‥‥たとえ、カイケツしてもぢゃ。
English: I'm afraid the damage the DL-6 Incident has done will never be eased...

Japanese: 御剣　信‥‥か。
English: Gregory Edgeworth...?

Japanese: 優秀なオトコぢゃった。 ‥‥ザンネンぢゃよ。
English: He was a gifted man. His death was truly a loss.

Japanese: 生きていれば、今、狩魔は どうなっていたことか‥‥。
English: I wonder what would have become of von Karma were he alive...

Japanese: 毎度まいどスマンが‥‥
English: Er... quite sorry.

Japanese: 特に思い当たることは ないようぢゃ‥‥。
English: I have nothing to say concerning that.

Japanese: １２月２７日　某時刻 管理人の小屋
English: December 27 Caretaker's Shack

Japanese: ‥‥誰もいないね。
English: Nobody's home.

Japanese: “オハヨー、オハヨー”
English: "Hello! Hello!" *squawk*

Japanese: あっ！　サユリさんだ！
English: Hey! It's Polly!

Japanese: あなたのご主人、 どこへ行っちゃったのかな‥‥。
English: I wonder where your owner's gone, Polly?

Japanese: “オハヨー、オハヨー”
English: "Hello! Hello!" *squawk*

Japanese: この子、置いていくなんて ヒドいよね！
English: I can't believe he'd run off and leave his poor parrot to fend for herself!

Japanese: “オハヨー、オハヨー”
English: "Hello! Hello!" *squawk*

Japanese: サユリさん、あたしたちが 引きとろうか。
English: Maybe I should take care of Polly, Nick?

Japanese: ‥‥勝手につれていっちゃ まずいよ。
English: You probably shouldn't just kidnap her.

Japanese: 警察の人たちも、こいつのことは 知ってるだろうから、
English: The police know about her anyway.

Japanese: なんとかしてくれるって。
English: I'm sure they'll do something.

Japanese: ‥‥ううう。
English: Well... okay...

Japanese: ゴメンね。サユリさん。 ダメだって。なるほどくんが。
English: Sorry, Polly. He says I can't take you.

Japanese: （ワルモノにされてしまった）
English: (Great, now the bird's going to hate me.)

Japanese: そういえばさ、なるほどくん。
English: That reminds me, Nick!

Japanese: この金庫の番号、サユリさんが 知ってるんだよね。
English: Polly here knows the number to the safe, right?

Japanese: ああ。そうだったね。
English: Yeah, that's right.

Japanese: サユリさん。金庫の番号は？
English: Polly, what's the number to the safe?
*squawk*

Japanese: ねえねえ、開けてみようよ。
English: Let's open it, Nick! C'mon!

Japanese: お金なんか入ってないと思うよ。
English: I'm sure there isn't any money in there.

Japanese: ううう‥‥。
English: Aww...

Japanese: でもさ。
English: But, hey!

Japanese: カギをかけているぐらいだから、 それなりのものが入ってるんだよ。
English: He keeps it locked, right? So there must be something of value in there!

Japanese: そうかなあ‥‥。
English: I'm not so sure...

Japanese: ほらほら、なるほどくん。 開ける開ける。
English: Okay, Nick, let's see what's in there!

Japanese: （‥‥何か手がかりが入ってるかも しれないからな‥‥）
English: (I guess there might be a clue or two...)

Japanese: 手紙が１通、入ってるだけだ。
English: The only thing in here is a letter.

Japanese: て、手紙？
English: A letter?

Japanese: つまんないの。
English: Aww... boring!

Japanese: （あて名も、差し出し人の 名前もない）
English: (Hmm... There's no name or signature on this thing.)

Japanese: （手書きの、きちょうめんな文字が きれいに並んでいる‥‥）
English: (It's handwritten in very precise, clear letters...)

Japanese: 《御剣　怜侍に復讐せよ‥‥》
English: "Get your revenge on Miles Edgeworth..."

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥ミツルギだと！
English: Edgeworth!

Japanese: なな、なるほどくん‥‥ なんで、御剣検事の名前が‥‥？
English: N-N-Nick! Why would Mr. Edgeworth's name be on here...?

Japanese: し、知らないよ！ ‥‥とにかく、読んでみるぞ。
English: How should I know!? I'm going to read the whole thing...!

Japanese: 《御剣　怜侍に復讐せよ‥‥》
English: "Get your revenge on Miles Edgeworth..."

Japanese: 《これが、最後のチャンスだ》
English: It also says: "This is your last chance!"

Japanese: 《お前の人生を破滅させた２人の 男に、今こそ復讐するのだ》
English: "Now is your time to get revenge on the two men who ruined your life!"

Japanese: （ここから、今回の殺人事件の 計画が、びっしり書かれている）
English: (The rest of the letter goes on to describe the murder plot in detail!)

Japanese: （生倉弁護士を殺害して、 その罪を御剣に着せる方法‥‥）
English: (How to kill Robert Hammond, and frame Edgeworth...)

Japanese: （御剣を呼び出し、ボートに乗り、 ピストルを２度、撃つ‥‥）
English: (...Calling Edgeworth out to the lake, getting on the boat... firing twice!)

Japanese: （今日の法廷で、ぼくが苦しまぎれ に説明したとおりのやり方が）
English: (This is exactly what I figured out today in court!)

Japanese: （カンペキに書かれている‥‥）
English: (It's all here... in perfect detail!)

Japanese: ど‥‥どういうこと？ なるほどくん‥‥。
English: What do you think it means, Nick?

Japanese: よくわからないけど、 どうやら、管理人のオジさん‥‥、
English: I don't know... but it looks like these are instructions for that caretaker.

Japanese: この手紙の指示どおりに動いて、 生倉弁護士を‥‥。
English: When he killed Robert Hammond and called out Edgeworth, he was following instructions!

Japanese: でも、その手紙‥‥。 いったい、誰が？
English: But who could have written that letter?

Japanese: それに、御剣　怜侍に復讐せよ、 って‥‥どういう意味なの？
English: And... what does it mean, to "get revenge on Miles Edgeworth"...?

Japanese: あのなあ。ぼくにわかるワケ ないだろ。
English: Look, I don't know, okay?

Japanese: ‥‥でも。
English: But one thing's for certain.

Japanese: どうやらこの手紙は スゴい手がかりになりそうだな。
English: This letter is an amazing clue!

Japanese: 《金庫の中の手紙》を、 法廷記録にそっと挟んだ。
English: Added Letter from the Safe to the Court Record.

Japanese: もう、金庫の中には 何も入っていないぞ。
English: There's nothing left in the safe.

Japanese: でも、オジさん、なんでこの手紙を 持って行かなかったのかな？
English: I wonder why the caretaker didn't take the letter with him?

Japanese: あわてて逃げたからだろ？ もどってきたようすもないし‥‥。
English: He left in a hurry, right? I don't think he even came back here after the trial.

Japanese: １２月２７日　某時刻 警察署内・資料室
English: December 27 Police Department Records Room

Japanese: ‥‥あいかわらず、 ホコリっぽいねー。
English: Dusty as always!

Japanese: あいかわらずも何も、 昨日来たばっかりだからなあ。
English: We were only here just yesterday. I'm sure they just haven't had time to clean...

Japanese: どうしたの？　なるほどくん？
English: What's wrong, Nick?

Japanese: ‥‥いや。 いないな、って思って。
English: Nothing. I was just noticing that he isn't here.

Japanese: 狩魔検事‥‥。
English: von Karma...

Japanese: あれ。ここだけ、開けっぱなしに なってるよ‥‥。
English: Huh? One of the drawers here is open.

Japanese: 最近、開けたみたいだね。
English: Someone must have been looking in it recently.

Japanese: 《未解決事件・証拠物件》って ラベルに書いてある‥‥。
English: The label says, "Unsolved Cases: Evidence"

Japanese: （《未解決事件》‥‥か）
English: Hmm... "Unsolved cases"?

Japanese: なるほどくん！
English: Nick!

Japanese: 《ＤＬ６号事件》のケースだけ、 カラっぽだよ！
English: The file for DL-6... it's completely empty!

Japanese: な‥‥なんだって！
English: Wh-what!?

Japanese: ‥‥そこで何をしている！
English: What are you doing in here!

Japanese: きゃあっ！
English: Eeek!

Japanese: かか、かるま検事‥‥。
English: v-v-von Karma!

Japanese: ‥‥キサマ‥‥
English: You...!

Japanese: なぜ、ワガハイの名を 知っている‥‥？
English: How do you know my name?

Japanese: え。
English: Huh?

Japanese: どこかで会ったことが？
English: Have we met?

Japanese: なな、ナニ言ってるんですか！
English: W-w-what are you saying!?

Japanese: 最近おなじみの カオじゃないですか！
English: We see each other every day, don't we?

Japanese: あたしたち、御剣　怜侍の 弁護士ですよ！
English: We're Miles Edgeworth's defense team!
Defense team...?

Japanese: これはシツレイ。
English: Ahem. I beg your pardon, you see, I rarely remember defense attorneys.

Japanese: 弁護士など、目に入らない ちっぽけな存在なものでな‥‥。
English: They are like bugs to me. Needless things, to be crushed.

Japanese: （うーん‥‥さすが御剣の 師匠だな‥‥）
English: (I can see how this guy was Edgeworth's mentor.)

Japanese: 大きな引き出しが 開きっぱなしになっている。
English: This large drawer is half-open.

Japanese: 引き出しのラベルの文字は、 《未解決事件・証拠物件》
English: The label on the drawer reads: "Unsolved Cases: Evidence."

Japanese: 大小の、ガラクタにしか見えない 証拠が、ひしめいている。
English: All the stuff in here looks like random junk.

Japanese: ＤＬ６号事件の証拠品だけ、 持ち出されている。
English: Only the evidence for the DL-6 Incident is missing.

Japanese: ‥‥もちろん、狩魔検事だろう。
English: I can guess who took it, too. von Karma.

Japanese: あ、あの‥‥御剣検事って、 お弟子さんなんですよね‥‥？
English: Uh, umm... Mr. Edgeworth was your student, right?

Japanese: ‥‥アマさが抜けない ロマンチストだ。
English: A romanticist who could never shed that veneer of amateurism.

Japanese: 父親と同じだ。 ‥‥一流にはなれん。
English: Just like his father. Always second rate.

Japanese: 狩魔検事。
English: Mr. von Karma...

Japanese: あなたは、御剣　信弁護士を ウラんでいますよね？
English: You had an axe to grind with Mr. Gregory Edgeworth, didn't you?

Japanese: 私が？　弁護士ごときを？ ‥‥なぜだ？
English: Me? A grudge against a mere defense attorney? Why?

Japanese: あなたのカンペキな経歴に キズをつけたからですよ。
English: Because he dealt a blow to your otherwise perfect trial record?
Hmph.

Japanese: それなのに、なぜ。
English: So you did... but what I don't get is...

Japanese: 御剣　怜侍を、検事として ここまで育てたんですか？
English: Why did you take his son under your wing afterwards?

Japanese: 憎いカタキのムスコなのに‥‥。
English: The son of your most bitter rival?

Japanese: キサマの知ったことではない。
English: That, my dear attorney, is none of your business.

Japanese: 明日の法廷は、最終日だ。
English: Tomorrow will be the last day of this trial.

Japanese: ‥‥ひさしぶりだ。最終日まで もちこたえた弁護士は。
English: It's been a while since I've had a defense attorney last this long.

Japanese: ‥‥しかし、結果は変わらない。
English: Still, you will lose in the end.

Japanese: 御剣　怜侍は明日、自らの 罪を認めることになるだろう。
English: Miles Edgeworth will admit his own guilt.

Japanese: “１５年前”の罪ですか‥‥？
English: His guilt of 15 years ago, you mean?

Japanese: 感心な勉強家だな。
English: You're quite the researcher.

Japanese: そこまで調べたのなら、 キサマにもわかるだろう。
English: If you've done your homework so well, then certainly, you must understand.

Japanese: 明日の法廷で、御剣　怜侍が 何を語るかを‥‥。
English: You know what Miles Edgeworth will tell the court tomorrow.

Japanese: （やはり‥‥）
English: (We were right...)

Japanese: （明日の事件で、コイツは ＤＬ６号事件のことを‥‥）
English: (So von Karma is going to bring up DL-6 in court tomorrow.)

Japanese: 狩魔検事。 ‥‥これを見てください。
English: Mr. von Karma, have a look at this.

Japanese: あなただったんですね‥‥。
English: This was you, wasn't it?

Japanese: 灰根　高太郎に、殺人の 指示を送ったのは‥‥。
English: You instructed Yanni Yogi to commit murder.

Japanese: 灰根　高太郎‥‥。
English: Yanni Yogi...

Japanese: あの男を、その名で呼ぶのを 聞くのは、何年ぶりだ‥‥。
English: How many years has it been since I've heard him called by that name.

Japanese: ‥‥バカな男だ。
English: He's a fool.

Japanese: 読んだら、必ず焼き捨てるよう 書いておいたのに‥‥。
English: I told him to burn it after he read it.

Japanese: み‥‥みとめるんですね！
English: S-so you admit it!

Japanese: あなたが‥‥あなたが、 灰根さんに、この手紙を‥‥！
English: You... you wrote Mr. Yogi this letter!

Japanese: ‥‥さて、弁護士。
English: Yes, my dear defense attorney.

Japanese: わざわざ、持ってきてくれて ありがとう。
English: Thank you for taking the trouble to bring it to me.

Japanese: おかげで、よけいな労力を はらわずにすんだよ‥‥。
English: You've saved me from a lot of needless hassle.

Japanese: な‥‥なんだと！
English: Wh-what!?

Japanese: な‥‥なるほどくん‥‥。 な、なに、あれ‥‥？
English: N-Nick! What is that thing!?

Japanese: ス‥‥スタンガンだよ。 護身用の武器だ。
English: A stun gun. For self defense... usually.

Japanese: そのとおり。
English: Indeed.

Japanese: ６０万ボルトの火花が キサマの身体にハジける‥‥。
English: 600,000 volts will course through your body like a dog touching an electric fence.

Japanese: ろ‥‥ろくじゅうまん‥‥。
English: Six hundred thous...!?

Japanese: 心配はいらない。 死にはしないからな‥‥たぶん。
English: Oh, don't worry. People don't die from it, usually.

Japanese: さあ。 わたしてもらおうか、手紙を。
English: Now, give me the letter.

Japanese: やめてええええええええええっ
English: Nooooooooooo!

Japanese: うおッ！　な、何をするかッ！
English: Whoa! What are you--!?

Japanese: な、なるほどくん、逃げて‥‥！
English: N-Nick! Run!!!

Japanese: きゃああああっ！
English: Aaaaaaaaaaaaah!

Japanese: ま‥‥真宵ちゃん！
English: M-Maya!

Japanese: 手こずらせおって！
English: Out of my way!

Japanese: うわああああっ！
English: Waaaaaaaaaaaah!

Japanese: （‥‥やられた‥‥）
English: (Ugh... He got us.)

Japanese: （手紙‥‥‥‥もちろん、 うばわれちまったなあ‥‥）
English: (The letter's... gone, of course.)

Japanese: （ＤＬ６号事件の証拠品も 持って行かれて‥‥）
English: (And he took the DL-6 evidence... all of it.)

Japanese: （手がかりはゼロ、か‥‥）
English: (Back to having no clues.)
(Wait... Maya jumped first.)

Japanese: （真宵ちゃんは、 だいじょうぶかな‥‥）
English: (Maya...! Is she okay?)

Japanese: ま‥‥真宵ちゃん！
English: Ma...Maya!

Japanese: おい！　しっかりしてくれよ！
English: Maya! Open your eyes!

Japanese: 真宵ちゃん！
English: Maya!

Japanese: ‥‥とられちゃった？　手紙‥‥
English: The letter! Did he take it?

Japanese: え‥‥あ、ああ。 それより、だいじょうぶ‥‥？
English: Huh!? Oh... yeah. A-are you okay!?

Japanese: ‥‥ホント‥‥ダメだね‥‥。
English: I... I couldn't stop him.

Japanese: イセイよく飛びかかっても、 １発でキゼツしちゃって‥‥。
English: I jumped as fast as I could, but one shot from that thing knocked me out cold.

Japanese: あたしなんて‥‥
English: I'm useless.

Japanese: お姉ちゃんを呼ぶことしか 能がない、ヤク立たずなのに‥‥
English: I'm no good as a lawyer, or a medium! I can't even call my sister.

Japanese: こんな‥‥一番ダイジなときに、 それもできない。
English: Not even now, when we need her the most.

Japanese: このまま‥‥目がさめなけりゃ よかったな‥‥。
English: I wish I hadn't woken up at all.

Japanese: 真宵ちゃん！
English: Maya!

Japanese: （クソッ！　なんとかして あげられないのか‥‥！）
English: (Grr! There has to be some way I can help her!)

Japanese: （とにかく、自信を回復 させる方法を探すしかないな‥）
English: (I'd better do something about her self-confidence, first.)

Japanese: （真宵ちゃん‥‥ 何か、にぎりしめているぞ）
English: (Maya... She's holding something!)

Japanese: （これは‥‥弾丸？）
English: (What is that? A bullet?)

Japanese: 《ＤＬ６号事件　証拠品７号 御剣　信の心臓部より摘出》
English: "DL-6 Incident, Evidence No. 7 Taken from the heart of Gregory Edgeworth."

Japanese: そうか‥‥。
English: I remember...

Japanese: 狩魔検事に飛びかかったとき、 夢中でこれをつかんだんだな‥‥。
English: von Karma was holding this when Maya jumped him.

Japanese: 《ＤＬ６号事件の弾丸》を、 ポケットにしまった。
English: Put DL-6 Bullet in pocket.

Japanese: （‥‥証明してやるよ、 真宵ちゃん）
English: (I'll prove it to you, Maya.)

Japanese: （きみは、 役立たずじゃない‥‥）
English: (You're most definitely not useless!)

Japanese: （何もかも、証明してやる。 ‥‥最後の法廷で‥‥！）
English: (I'll prove it to you in court tomorrow!)

Japanese: つづく
English: To be continued.

Japanese: ‥‥おろかな。
English: Fool.

Japanese: 検事が、弁護士に対して 情報を与えると思うか‥‥。
English: You think I, a prosecutor, would give you, a defense attorney, information? Bah!
(Creep!)

Japanese: “ひょうたん湖　自然公園” と書いてある。
English: The sign says "Gourd Lake Nature Park."

Japanese: 休日は、家族づれでにぎわう、 都会のオアシスといったところだ。
English: This place is full of families picnicking on the weekend.

Japanese: でも、滝はないんだよね。
English: But... no waterfall.

Japanese: 都会の人間は、 修行はしないからね。
English: Not many picnickers come here for spiritual training, Maya.

Japanese: 葉を落とした木々に、 冬の訪れを感じる今日このごろ。
English: I feel winter's chill from the bare leaf trees today...

Japanese: うーん。冬は人を 詩人にさせるなあ。
English: *sigh* What is it about winter that turns people into poets?

Japanese: ねー、サムいよなるほどくん。 早くどこか行こうよ。
English: I don't know, but my toes are starting to feel numb.

Japanese: （サムいって、ぼくの詩か？）
English: (Yes... my poetry has that effect on some people.)

Japanese: こーんなにサムいのに、ベンチに 座る人、いないよねー。
English: No one's going to sit here on a cold day like today.

Japanese: まあ、おマンジュウがあれば 別だけどさ。
English: Well, unless they were eating a Samurai Dog!

Japanese: （マンジュウがあるのとないのとで 状況はどう変わるのだろう？）
English: (How would that change the temperature, I wonder?)

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥？ なに、むずかしいカオしてるの？
English: You seem troubled, Nick.

Japanese: べ、べつに‥‥。
English: N-no, who? Me?

Japanese: 妙に目立たない道しるべが 立っている。
English: Huh... I almost didn't see that signpost.

Japanese: “左‥‥ボートのりば” “右‥‥公園出口”
English: "Left - Boat Docks" "Right - Exit"

Japanese: それにしても、メーワクな 湖だね、ひょうたん湖。
English: This lake sure likes to cause problems, doesn't it.

Japanese: 何が？
English: Huh?

Japanese: ここで起こったこと、 ぜーんぶウソだったもんね。
English: I mean, everything that happened here turned out to be a lie!

Japanese: ヒョッシーもウソ、御剣検事の 殺人容疑もウソ。
English: Gourdy was a lie, and the charges against Mr. Edgeworth were all lies.

Japanese: そうだなあ。
English: I guess you're right.

Japanese: ‥‥まあ、ウソでよかったけどね。
English: I mean, I'm glad the charges were all lies, but still.

Japanese: 矢張のヤツ、今日は お休みか‥‥。
English: Guess Larry has today off.

Japanese: 浮かれてるからね。 ‥‥御剣検事を助けて。
English: He was pretty happy about saving Mr. Edgeworth.

Japanese: たしかに‥‥。アイツには カンシャしなきゃな。
English: True. We owe him big.

Japanese: ゴミは入っていない。
English: A trash can with no trash.

Japanese: この公園は、管理のよさが ウリだからな‥‥。
English: At least the place is well maintained.

Japanese: 貸しボート屋の管理小屋だ。 人のケハイはない。
English: The boat rental shop. Doesn't look like anyone is around.

Japanese: 管理人のオジさん、 やっぱり逃げちゃったのかな‥‥。
English: The caretaker must have run for the hills, huh?

Japanese: ああ、たぶんね‥‥。
English: Yeah, looks like it.

Japanese: いい人そうに 見えたのになあ‥‥。
English: He didn't seem like a bad person...

Japanese: このシゲミの奥は、 森になっている。
English: There's a forest here beyond these bushes.

Japanese: なるほどくん！　森の中、 誰かいるみたいだよ！
English: Nick! The forest! There's someone in there!

Japanese: ホントだ。‥‥何人もの 警官のすがたが見える。
English: You're right. There's a few policemen in there.

Japanese: あのオジさんを探してるんだ。
English: They must be looking for the caretaker!

Japanese: タイヘンだな‥‥。
English: Good luck...

Japanese: 湖の上に、ボートが浮かんでいる。
English: There are some boats floating at the dock.

Japanese: 殺人事件は、このボートの上で 起こったわけか‥‥。
English: The murder took place in a boat from this dock...

Japanese: 実際に事件が起こったボートは、 警察が持ち帰ったようだ。
English: Apparently, the police took away the actual boat that was used that night.

Japanese: ボートが並んでいる場所に、 １そうぶんのスペースが空いてる。
English: Indeed, there's space for one more boat at the dock.

Japanese: 当然のことながら、 こたつは冷えている。
English: Hmm. Everything's cold. Looks like he didn't turn his heater on.

Japanese: オジさん、ここには帰って きていないようだ。
English: I guess he hasn't been back here since the trial.

Japanese: このキッチン、 あんまり使ってないみたい。
English: It doesn't look like he used this kitchen much.

Japanese: そうだね。
English: You're right.

Japanese: おソバ屋さんていうの、 ウソだったのかな、やっぱり。
English: I guess the whole pasta restaurant thing was a lie.

Japanese: ‥‥本当だった方が びっくりするけどなあ。
English: What, you thought he was telling the truth!?

Japanese: あのさ、なるほどくん。
English: Say, Nick.

Japanese: 魚拓って、“こんな大きいサカナを 釣ったぞ！”っていうことでしょ？
English: Don't people usually put pictures of fish up on the wall to boast about them?

Japanese: よくわからないけど、たしかに ジマンみたいなもんだろうね。
English: Uh, yeah, I guess so. You mean pictures of the fish they caught, right?

Japanese: でもさ。なんか、セコいサカナ ばっかりだよ、このへん。
English: Right... but don't all the fish on the wall here look really puny to you?

Japanese: ‥‥まあ、“ジマン”てのは、 “ジコマンゾク”の略だからね。
English: Well, you know what they say...
"You shoulda seen the one that got away!"

Japanese: へええ！　‥‥そうなんだ。
English: Except the one that got away from us was the caretaker! And we did see him!

Japanese: （感心されても困るけど‥‥）
English: (Why do I feel like we're having two different conversations here?)

Japanese: ‥‥あッ！
English: Aah!

Japanese: ど、どうした？
English: W-what's wrong?

Japanese: ‥‥え？　う、ううん。 な、なんでもない‥‥。
English: Huh? Oh... N-never mind.

Japanese: なんだよ。気になるなあ。
English: What? Tell me!

Japanese: あのね。テレビ見たら 思い出したんだけど‥‥、
English: Just, when I saw the TV, I remembered...

Japanese: 今週、ヒメサマンの スペシャル番組があるな、って。
English: They're showing a Pink Princess special this week...

Japanese: なんだ。
English: Oh.

Japanese: うう‥‥だから言いたく なかったのに‥‥。
English: See? That's why I didn't want to tell you.

Japanese: 高そうなツリザオだ。
English: That fishing pole looks expensive.

Japanese: イトノコ刑事に 持っていってあげようか。
English: Maybe we should bring it to Detective Gumshoe.

Japanese: いやいや、いくらなんでも オジさんに悪いだろ？
English: Don't you think the caretaker would mind?

Japanese: じゃあさ。かわりに金属探知機を 置いておけばいいよ。
English: Well, we can just leave him the metal detector in exchange!

Japanese: ‥‥それは違うだろう。
English: Uh... maybe we'd better not.

Japanese: 警察のマスコットキャラクター ‥‥って、こんなんだったっけ？
English: Hey, is that the police department's mascot...?

Japanese: “タイホくん”だよ。
English: That's the "Blue Badger"!

Japanese: ワシが考えて、ワシが作った。 ワシだけのマスコットさ。
English: It was my idea! I made it! It's my mascot!

Japanese: そ、そうなんですか。
English: I... see. How nice.

Japanese: いよいよ明日、署長にプレゼンする つもりだよ。
English: Tomorrow, I'll present him before the department at last!

Japanese: いかにこの“タイホくん”が 愛らしいかを、聞いてもらうんだ。
English: I'm going to make sure they know how much people like the Blue Badger!

Japanese: ‥‥がんばってください。
English: Um, good luck!

Japanese: 刑事さんだろうか。
English: That must be one of the detectives.

Japanese: ‥‥何やら、ひとりでぶつぶつ つぶやいている。
English: He's mumbling something to himself.

Japanese: “たのむ！　考えなおしてくれ、 ヨシコ‥‥”
English: "Please! Think about what you're doing, Jolinda!"

Japanese: “タカシまでつれていくなんて、 あんまりだぜ‥‥！”
English: "Don't take my Tommy away from me! No!"

Japanese: 離婚のシュラ場のイメージ・ トレーニングをしているようだ。
English: He must be doing image training for a nasty divorce argument.

Japanese: 刑事さんだろうか。
English: That must be one of the detectives.

Japanese: ‥‥何やら、ひとりでぶつぶつ つぶやいている。
English: He's mumbling something to himself.

Japanese: “あの‥‥ごシュミは‥‥？”
English: "So, um, what are your hobbies?"

Japanese: “‥‥はあ。 張り込みと尾行を少々‥‥”
English: "Well... I like to do stake-outs now and then..."

Japanese: お見合いのイメージ・トレーニング をしているようだ。
English: He must be doing image training for a first date.

Japanese: 刑事課の課長さんだろう。
English: This must be the chief of the detectives here.

Japanese: ‥‥パソコンの画面を、 食いいるように見つめている。
English: He's glued to his computer screen.

Japanese: なにぃ‥‥！ な、なんということだ‥‥！
English: Wha...!? Th-that's not possible!

Japanese: 昨日で、世界が終わっていただ なんて‥‥
English: The world ended... yesterday!?

Japanese: （‥‥くだらないホームページの 予言を、真に受けてるみたいだ）
English: (He must be reading predictions for the future on someone's homepage.)

Japanese: 刑事たちの机がならんでいる。
English: These are the detectives' desks.

Japanese: パソコンに、資料。 ‥‥思ったより片づいているな。
English: There are computers and files on each one. Funny, they're a lot tidier than I'd expect.

Japanese: たぶん、あまり席にいることが ないんだろう。
English: I guess the detectives don't spend a lot of time at their desks.

Japanese: 婦人警官がビシッと 敬礼している。
English: A poster of a female police officer...

Japanese: ‥‥と思ったら、制服を着た アイドルのグラビアだった。
English: Wait, no. That's the latest "Babes in Uniform" calendar. My bad.

Japanese: ガラスケースの中には、 ファイルが並んでいるみたいだ。
English: It looks like there are files inside this glass case.

Japanese: ガラスに厚くホコリが かぶっているから、よく見えない。
English: The case is so dusty I can't see what's inside.

Japanese: なるほどくん。 ‥‥カギもかかってるよ。
English: Nick... it's locked.

Japanese: 大事な資料がしまってある みたいだな‥‥。
English: They must keep important case files in there.

Japanese: このキャビネットには、 証拠品が保管されているようだ。
English: This cabinet is where they keep evidence for current cases.

Japanese: あきらかに凶器とわかるものから、 “なんだこりゃ”的なものまで、
English: Some of the things are obviously murder weapons others... are question marks.

Japanese: ガラクタにしか見えない証拠が ひしめいている。
English: Most of it just looks like random junk.

Japanese: なるほどくん。 なあに、この洗濯バサミ？
English: Nick... what do you think this clothespin is for?

Japanese: だから、さわるなって。 ‥‥いちおう、証拠品だから。
English: Don't touch that. It's evidence...

Japanese: 事件の調書をまとめた ファイルだ。
English: Here are files of collected case reports.

Japanese: ‥‥それにしても、 ものすごい量だな‥‥。
English: There's quite a large volume of reports here.

Japanese: ふえー。‥‥これ、全部 事件のことが書いてあるの？
English: Wow... these are all case reports!?

Japanese: そうだよ。ここは、事件の墓場 みたいなもんだから。
English: Yeah. It's like a graveyard of police cases.

Japanese: お姉ちゃんの事件も、ここで ホコリをかぶっていくんだね‥‥。
English: I guess my sister's case report is in here too... quietly gathering dust.

Japanese: 部屋の奥にも、ぎっしりファイルが 詰まった棚が並んでいる。
English: There are shelves stuffed with case files in the back of the room, too.

Japanese: 誰もなつかしむことのない、 事件の亡きがらたちだ。
English: Forgotten cases, rotting away for eternity...

Japanese: なるほどくん。早く 用事をすませちゃおうよ。
English: Nick, let's get what we need and get out of here.

Japanese: 肺の中、まっクロに なっちゃうよー！
English: All this dust is getting to me!
